I'm not a huge fan of triple chocolate cakes because they are usually too rich, but a narrow sliver of this is perfect.  Loved it, and thank you so much for rescuing this one!  
-Caryn
Wow is all I can say.  I made a heart shaped cake yesterday and it was divine!  Took longer to bake because I used one 6" deep pan.  The only chocolate cake recipe I will ever need!
Fabulous!  The only way I can see to improve this recipe is to use unsalted butter or ghee in place of the bottled oil.
I made this chocolate cake for my little boy's birthday and it went down a storm! I followed one of the reviewers and used the frosting suggested. It was lovely! Thank you so much.

I have made this 4 times now all with in about a month's time. Each time it is perfect. The only thing that is bothering me is that each time I've mixed the cocoa powder and boiling water it has given me a different consistency...but no matter what the cake is awesome each time. Thanks!

Yummy!!
I was afraid they wouldn't rise because the batter was so thin, but they were perfect. Although next time I will fill the muffin tins fuller, because they don't grow hugely like some cupcakes. 
The flavor was wonderful. just the perfect amount of chocolate and not too heavy. 
I frosted with kitten kal's white fluffy frosting. Wonderful combination!

This is a great cake... the only thing I did was change the temperature from 350 degrees to 325 degrees F.  This keeps my cakes from getting that dome on top and they are flat when they are baked.  Of course, I had to bake this cake for 45 minutes, but it was perfectly flat when I turned it out onto the cooling rack.  I use the toothpick method to make sure it is done... do not leave it in until the cake pulls from the sides as it will be dry.  Everyone was right about the taste of the cake!  Thanks for sharing.

I DOUBLED this recipe and poured it into 2 11 inch cake pans for a big wedding shower; it was a HIT! I used a chocolate frosing fililng #44126 and then used a white (it's more bridal, right?!?) frosting on the outside, #80118. THANKS!
Made for hubby's birthday. Easy, moist, chocolatey. Very good. Will make again.Thanks.

This is the chocolate cake recipe I've been looking for. Rich, dark and perfectly moist. This is the taste you hope for when you see the big slices of cake in restaurants or magazine ads. This is my chocolate cake recipe from now on. Thanks.

Wow, I HATE giving out low star reviews but for whatever reason, there was WAY to much liquid in this cake. Before I even added the oil, water or milk, it was the consistency of a a cake mix so I was wondering why I should add more liquid. After 45 minutes, the cake still wasn't solid. I could see the oil boiling on the top! Eeek! I finally pulled it out of the oven, it was in a silicone dinousar mold because it was going to be a birthday cake. I turned it over to get it out and it collapsed in an oily mess. I'm going to try to freeze what came out and turn it into a trifle but I don't know if that will workl. Sorry :(

Great for high altitudes!  Perfectly moist, held together just fine, incredibly easy to make and basically a one-bowl recipe.  This is the only chocolate cake recipe I use now, period.  It is so delicious I even like it without frosting.
Gud cake nuthing dat great though zaar has better recipes that i wud look upto .This cake is not very sweet so a sweeter icing wud fit the perfect sweeth tooths like me !!

This is a fantastic chocolate cake.  It's very moist, dense but tender, and deeply chocolate.  As some other reviewers noted, it gets more moist as it sits so is best made a day ahead of serving.  I spread a thick layer of white chocolate ganache betwen the layers, and covered the ganache with a raspberry filling.  The tart raspberries paired well with the sweet ganache.  I covered the top of the cake with a thick layer of bittersweet chocolate ganache, and frosted the sides with a rich chocolate buttercream.  Because this cake isn't as sweet as some, you can lather on the frostings and fillings.  My layers were done in 29 minutes.  I dusted the pans with cocoa instead of flour.  To enhance the chocolate flavour, I added 1 tbsp. of instant espresso to the boiling water.

Excellent recipe!  I had everything on hand except the buttermilk.  Made that by adding 1 T. lemon juice to 1 c. milk.  This cake will definitely replace my old stand by recipe. Topped it with recipe #44126.  Love this cake!  Thanks ms_bold for posting here!

Delicious! I halved the recipe since I only needed one 9" round cake. I used 2 oz. unsweetened chocolate squares plus 1 Tbsp. Hershey's cocoa, reduced the oil a tiny bit since I used chocolate squares and it turned out perfectly. Baked for 35 min at 350.

My son made this cake for a youth fair and won first prize! It's such a moist, delicious cake, and the directions are easy to follow, even for a beginner. He greased the pans and then coated with cocoa powder instead of flour, and skipped the parchment paper. Thanks ms bold for adopting!
The perfect chocolate cake, dark and wonderful.

I have made this cake several time now, and it is quite good.  However, unlike the description, I would not consider it a "dense" cake. It is more dense than a box mix by a little, but not as dense as several other home made cake recipes I have tried over the years.  It is very moist, and makes a HUGE cake.  In fact, I now cut the recipe back to 2/3 of the original volume most of the time.  I have also found it takes longer than 30 min to bake.  Thanks for a good base recipe.

Needed a cake to impress the boyfriend and his family for his birthday, and this definitely did the trick.  Preparation was easy and the cake rose to perfection for a dual layer cake in exactly 30 minutes.  Flavor was good (not too sweet), cake was moist, and it turned out perfectly.
This cake was great! I used almond milk and vinegar instead of buttermilk, and whole wheat flour, plus I added some chocolate chips. Easy to make and really moist, I will be making this again.
Delicious chocolate cake, and so easy to make. I'll be making this again, thank you!

I made this for my daughter's birthday. Absolutely loved it. Very moist and rich flavor. I used Kittencals Chocolate frosting with the cake. The combination was delicious.  I liked the suggestion of adding mini-cholocate chips. Will try that next time.  Can never get enough chocolate!

Very good!  My 9-year old and I made this for his birthday.  We used regular milk as we didn't have buttermilk on hand and somehow we forgot the vanilla....but it still turned out fantastic!  Will definitely be using this one again, it is a keeper, and seemingly fail proof!

This recipe created a cake light, moist and fluffy exactly what I was looking for to create a special cake for my mother in law.  I love that I didn't have to cream the butter etc and I just used a whisk for the whole lot. I reduced the sugar a quarter and the runniness worried me but it cooked perfectly. I changed the amounts to make 3 layers and they were quite thick. The centre of this cake rises so I sliced the tops off to make even layers. I'll be using this recipe again, it was a huge hit and got great comments. I won't need another chocolate cake recipe. Thanks for posting
This is a good recipe when pressed for time and ingredients.  I might add more seasoning salt to give the flavor a little more oomph.

Thanks Parsley for another great recipe.....The spinach mixture had a lot of liquid so I poured off the excess and added 1/4 cup plain bread crumbs. I also added 3 cloves of garlic instead of two  WOW..I will use 2 next time (my error) but I love garlic!! I asked my son how many stars and before I could finish my sentence he said "5"....I agree :)
Made for PAC spring '09

I used this recipe for the marinade, on a pork loin roast.  It was awesome!!!  My family loved it!  I will defiantly have to try with the chicken.  Thank you for posting a fantastic recipe! 
I recommend for all to try!

I knew these were going to be awesome when I mixed the meat up with all the spices - it smelled so good!  And I was very happy with how they turned out.  Great, bold, complex flavors, and the ground chicken (I used that instead of turkey) gave these a much nicer texture than ground beef patties.  I had several guests over, and we all agreed that these were wonderful.  I'll be making these again for certain!  Thanks!  Made for PAC Spring 2010.
this really is a chicken/turkey burger not patty- most people think of that as one that is breaded and then cooked.

I really liked these, and so did my kiddos (4yo and 1yo). I didn't have any coconut, so that was not added, and I had a lonely plum in the fridge, so that was peeled, chopped, and added to the mix along with the other fruits. I also did not have to bake these as long; 25 minutes was enough.
I'd like to try these again, with brown sugar for half of the sugar called for.

What an easy to prepare fish soup!  This is loaded with flavors that work well together.  I cooked the vegetables in the white wine for a few minutes before I added the water.     There was a lovely aroma in the kitchen...I will definitely make this again!
I used white candy melts and made this really bright green.  It's wonderful!  I think next time I would like to add some flavoring or amaretto to it just for fun.

I'm the sister that made this recipe for diner524 for her birthday!  We were perusing her Barbeque Bible for recipes for the Zaar tour and came across this one.  It was delicious!  And very simple to make.  I know I will make this many times!  Thanks for a great recipe Sis!
Delicious and so easy!! I added some chili peppers to my marinade and used a London Broil that I had purchased at the market on sale. It was great! Thank you!

I would give this 10 stars if I could!!  I've been trying a whole slew of new recipes, so I haven't been repeating much, but this.  This I have made a few times already!  I use beef tenderloin, but it is somewhere between 1 and 1 1/2 lb (I cut up whole tenderloins and freeze).  I've doubled the marinade before so that everything gets fully soaked, and marinate about 1 hour.  And I actually haven't tried grilling yet, so I can attest to the fact that it is wonderful cooked on the stovetop!
The instructions for this recipe are unclear (how much cottage cheese?). Try boosting it up a bit with the use of some herbs and spices.

Are you kidding...nobody's rated this?  This is awesome.  I've made it several times and every time it's received with ooohhhs and aawwws.  This is fantastic.  Thank you for something besides Green Giant in Butter Sauce (my midnight snack sometimes so I didn't have to share with my children...lol).  Thank you for sharing.  I serve it with a awesome marinated steak (rib eyes preferably) and add bread/rolls if you wish.  Bon Appetit! :)
I loved it! Will add a little more lemon next time.
I love this soup, I put a little soup base in for extra flavor, it is easy and my customers LOVE it! The orzo stays much better than rice.  Great flavor, Great soup!
I made this cordial.  We loved the sweet tartness of this drink.  It is reallt good on vanilla ice cream.
I doubled this recipe for a party this weekend and the gals sucked both pitchers dry! They were drinking glassfuls of it with ice, and loving every bit of it! Thank you for posting!
This was a very satisfying soup and I love the toast. I added cinnamon and nutmeg.

This was met by rave reviews from my 4 yr old DD. Between the two of us we finished the whole pan in one day! The only thing I wasn't crazy about was the crispness of the onions. They seemed underdone to me, even though they were chopped very fine. I think next time I will saute the onions first to soften them up a little, and also maybe add a little salt to balance out the sweetness of the cornbread. Great fun recipe.

I just made this for dinner and it was great.  I never found a good way to make pork and this is perfect.  The pork was so tender and the potatoes were sooo flavorful.  I went with the chicken brother instead of using the water. Thanks for sharing!

This was a lovely and tasty recipe! Quite simple but lots of flavor! I had forgotten about thickening broth with flour instead of using fat in a white sauce or gravy. It was also nice to not have cream of something soup. I did add some extra spices, just my personal preference such as garlic and onion powder, and a dash of red pepper flakes, and a bit of paprika on top of the chops. I enjoyed mashing my potatoes with my fork into the onion broth. I might thicken this just a tad more next time and think that it would transfer well to the crockpot too.
I have made paneer before but it crumbled and was difficult to cut.  The step with the food processor made all the difference.  Thanks for that tip!

I love larb gai and I made this recipe with a few adjustments (more cilantro, less fish sauce and thai chili's and sambal paste instead of cayenne). I also use red onions. This is so delicious and it is nice to see a recipe that actually uses lemongrass, rice powder and the kaffir lime leaves. That makes world of difference! Delicious and even better the second day..Thanks!

oh baby, oh baby, oh baby ... this was wonderful. to keep it low carb, I left out the rice powder and substituted liquid sucralose for the sugar. and leftover? throw it in the food processor with some celery and mayo - fabulous thai-flavored chicken salad.

This is excellent!  I love ordering larb gai at my Thai restaurant, and this was easy to make at home.  I used lime zest instead of kaffir leaves, and instead of wrapping in fresh cabbage, I served the chicken salad on a bed of wilted chopped cabbage.  Thanks for the excellent recipe - this is a keeper!

The cookies were good. The texture was nice, it is almond flour. They were easy to make. I didn't refrigerate them, just droped and flattened with moist fingers. The dough was sticky, but no problem.
For a diabetic cookie I'm excited about all the ways I can alter the flavorings. I baked them at 325 for 12 minutes.

Made last night for hubby's Christmas potluck and of course had to try some before I put in on the plate!  I made as posted (well, perhaps added a bit more Kahlua - about a quarter cup more!!).  Only slight issue was that cake was really, really thick and it did not cook in 45 minutes, it took much longer, about 60 mins or so, but needed it - it was not burnt or anything.  Perhaps my oven? but this was only minor issue.  Otherwise, great cake!
This was great!  Super easy to make and delicious.

I can't believe this recipe doesn't have any reviews yet.  As far as I'm concerned, this is one of ICM's best recipes, right up there with Mexican hot chocolate snicker doodles.  Yum!  This recipe is easy to throw together and basically fool-proof.  The blondie is moist and chewy with just the right amount of crunch around the edges, and the topping is divine.  Whether you're vegan or not, this is a to-die-for goodie!

This is a lovely, delicate sorbet. I did omit the egg, and reduced the sugar a bit. It is perfect for a summern nigt. Also I had it one night in a glass, with a shot of vodka poured over it....very nice!

This is great! I didn't have any onion salt on hand, so did a search on Zaar and came up with this recipe.Now I have onion salt and saved some money as well. Thanks for the great recipe! I will definitely use this again!

I loved this recipe.  Next time, I will increase the intensity  of the coffee.  The recipe was confusing as it omitted tht adding of the sugar, but I added it with the brown sugar, as I think was intended.  I love the taste.  Next time I will also add some nuts. Thanks for posting.

My son said, "Mom, this is the BEST dessert you've ever made! I will definitely make this again. Thank you for sharing the recipe! I made it as the recipe states (but with Hawaiian bread), and mine puffed up like a souffle also. Refreshing and delicious with fresh peaches. :)
Delicious and so easy! Just perfect for a romantic Valentine's Dinner!
Fantastic!  I am not diabetic but I love to try recipes with little or no sugar content.  This pie was wonderful and very easy to prepare.  Thank you!

Wonderful if you're looking to avoid sugar! 

I made this without the crust and everyone scraped their dish clean. 

I do recommend refrigerating it for at least an hour so the jello can set. One time I made it I didn't have time to and I was hoping the jello would set up anyway. It didn't, but everyone still ate it. 

Excellent recipe! Thank you!

I made this for my uncle who is a newly-diagnosed diabetic. This was extremely sweet for my taste. My uncle said he liked it, though he only took a few bites; mostly just the crust. I used diet sprite, 4cups of strawberries and sugar-free lady fingers. I didn't have time to make a pie crust and couldn't find a ready-made graham crust that is sugar free. I thought the lady fingers were a good substitute as it subdued the sweetness a bit. Since there is no way to control the sweetness in this recipe, unfortunately, I won't make it again.

I make this for my husband because he is  diabetic and it is 1 of his fav's...I love it too!!!! I have served it to many guest and they never have a clue that it is sugar-free and it is loved by all!!!Thanx for sharing this wonderful recipe....

Good comfort food!  I made a few changes.  I didn't have small potatoes, so I cut mine into slices about 1/4 inch thick.  I found that it took a bit longer than the 10 minutes cooking time to get the potatoes tender enough to suit me.  I covered the skillet and steamed them a bit while they were cooking, and ended up cooking the potatoes for about 20 minutes before adding the mushrooms.  I also left out the cherry tomatoes, as I don't like them.  I couldn't find any herbed sour cream at my local grocery, so I used regular sour cream.  No fresh thyme, so I just sprinkled a goodly portion of dried thyme into the sour cream.  The thyme gave this dish a very nice flavor, a little different from plain old potatoes.  Good recipe, and one I'll use again.  Thanks for sharing!

This is a spectacular dish - very elegant and yet still hearty if that makes sense.  The calvados really makes the flavor.   This is one of the best dinners I have made in a while - thank you for posting this recipe.
Tangy, flavorful, simple.  A great way to start the day.  I added walnuts and flax seeds.  Added it to plain instant oatmeal.
I really liked how this wasn't too sweet...just right!  I used frozen berries which instantly cooled the oatmeal down to the right eating temperature.  Thanks Charlotte J!
This recipe was great!  My family really liked it.  I used regular oats and increased the cooking time to 5 minutes, otherwise I went pretty much by the recipe (I did put in a little extra creamer).

I wanted to use up some of the fresh blueberries I had and make something my 15 month old would enjoy. I decided to try this recipe since he loves oatmeal. However, I did make some modifications.  I only added 2 tablespoons of sugar and instead of the french vanilla non-dairy coffee creamer, I used a little heavy cream and whole milk and added a splash of vanilla.  My son loves the oatmeal!  I tried it too, and it is delicious!  Thank you for sharing!

These were simple and fun to make. I used a small plastic bag with the corner cut off as a piping bag. 
I don't know if my almond essence wasn't very strong but I had to add about a teaspoon to get a good almond flavour. 
The biscuits were nice and crunchy on the edge but chewy in the middle - and didn't last long!

I just got a springform pan and was very excited to use it for the first time. I used light cream cheese, low-fat graham crackers and a light butter substitute and this cheesecake was still wonderful. I didn't have a lime so I increased the orange and lemon rind. I used bottled lime juice and fresh lemon and orange juice. I placed an orange and a lemon wedge in the center for effect. I'll be keeping this recipe for future use. Thanks for sharing it.

Anna, thanks for sharing this recipe. i love cheesecakes and i'm always on the lookout for unusual and delicious recipes. this recipe is a winner!! so good and refreshing...i think i might make it again this weekend :)
I loved this!  I used whatever greens and veggies I had on hand. The dressing was made just as stated...boy do I love avocados!  Thanks for sharing!

I made this for a get-together (quadrupled), and people couldn't stop raving about the dressing! I reduced the olive oil to 1/4c and added in a few dashes of red wine vinegar. It was excellent. I will make this my new salad dressing; I love that its bright and healthy and there are no nasty preservatives or additives.
This dressing rocks!!  Wow! I love it! Easy to whip up and healthy.  After finishing my salad, I drizzled some on my salmon. Perfect combination! Try it!! Thanks for sharing this awesome recipe!

This salad dressing is great! Just made a regular garden salad (using baby romaine, shredded carrot, cucumber, tomato, chives, yellow bell pepper, and added some chopped mint in this time), and spooned the dressing over top.

The flavour of the salad dressing is awesome. I made it with 1/2 and 1/2 canola oil and olive oil, a dash of seasoning salt. Excellent!

Great little one dish meal!!! I only made 2 chicken breast, but kept all the other ingredients the same and after I cooked the pasta I just tossed it with the chicken and sauce.  I served with garlic breadsticks and it was a winner for my family!!!  Next time I make it I think I will add some steamed broccoli and maybe some bacon bits or something!  Delicious! Thanks for sharing!!!

Just by reading the ingredients.. it's wrong.  Yes, tres leches has 3 milks..but you are forgetting an important milk.. the goat's milk.

Addendum to the person that made the comment... I did make this AS DIRECTED by the recipe.  It didn't taste right.  BEFORE I rated the recipe. For fairness!  However, I made it again with the goat's milk it makes a HUGE difference with the richness of the recipe.  I lived 6 years throughout Central and Southern America and that was how they made it.. yes tres leches means "3 milks" (I do speak 7 dialects of Spanish) but it was not right for MY tastebuds
Were these ever good!!!  Made for a get together and everyone asked for the recipe.  The hardest part was dicing the Velveeta!  :)  Thanks for sharing your recipe!
This was a wonderful change from the same old banana bread.  It was moist and had wonderful flavor.  I doubled the recipe to use up leftover banana's and it came out perfect!
I made this for my grandparents and they simply loved it. I doubled the recipe and made 6 1/2 mini loaves, they turned out great. Thanks for posting :)

I've made a lot of banana bread in my time but I had never used coconut and pineapple.  Great addition and I will be making this often.  I used macadamia nuts and it was very good.  Thanks for a great banana bread mikekey!!

I made this into 12 large muffins, which took about 25 minutes to bake. They were moist, dense and very tasty. I used unsweetened coconut and there was still plenty of sweetness. I think a little rum flavouring would also be a nice addition. Thanks! Made for ZWT3.
Yummy --- a nice change from regular banana bread --- a friend of mine had some and said it was sorta like a Pina Colada!

I made muffins and got 18 medium sized ones.  I accidentally used a small bag of sliced almonds instead of the walnuts and I also used unsweetened coconut.  They turned out beautifully and tasted delicious.  All of the kids loved them.  Thanks for posting such a yummy tropical treat!

This bread is tooooo sweet. I made two loaves, one with all-purpose flour and the other with Gluten Free Flour. Both were good, but both were too sweet. All of my kids thought they were good, but we did not taste any of the spices. All we tasted was a little banana and a lot of sweet!
I may try this again, but I will cut the sugar down to 1/3 - 1/2 cup.. maybe even less.

The whole house smelled incredible while the bread was baking.  I used sour cream as I did not have yogurt.  I also used macadamia nuts to give it more of a tropical flavor.  I could hardly taste the pineapple or the spices.  I found it to be a dense banana bread, heavier than other banana breads I have made, but still very good.

In retrospect, there was an aftertaste that I just cannot forget.....too much baking soda totally destroys the chop flavor and I had to, unfortunately, dispose of the chops, although they were beautifully brown and tender.
Recipe turned out dry and lacked flavor.  I don't think I'll be making this one again.

I prepared these as a fun and festive treat to take to a 4th of July cookout tonight!  Super easy....just requires plenty of room in the fridge for chilling....and the tequila and liquor syrup added a touch of sweetness and subtle flavor to the melon.  (No real alcohol bite that I can discern.)  No doubt, they will be a big hit.  Thanks for sharing!

I made these last night when my sweet tooth kicked in. I used strawberries in mine.  We really enjoyed them, although I did top each one with a dab of whipped cream before popping into my mouth!  Cindy
Really good but burns quickly when cooked on the BBQ. Next time, I will use the oven.
The mayonnaise gives the corn a horrible taste.  Just butter is far better.

Divine! I made this for a friend's birthday and she absolutely loved it! The cake was moist & light and the frosting was delicious as well. I used a high quality Apricot jam as the filling instead and it was wonderful. Thank you so much for this recipe.

Wow!! Beautiful cake, texture and flavour. Looks really pretty when done. I added 1 1/4 cup sweetened coconut to the batter and 1/2 cup sweetened coconut on top of the cake when iced. The long coconut flakes look and taste better(personal preference). I also baked in a springform pan un-covered for 25 mins and covered for the rest of the time.  It took about 40-45 mins in my oven. I adjusted the icing and used 2 cups of icing sugar. I used raspberry jam as the filling. Pick a really good quality jam you love, the quality impacts the cake a great deal.  Next time I will use un-sweetened, seedless raspberry jam from Safeway(my favourite). Guests said the cake tasted light. Next time I might bake the jam as a layer in the cake. This would also make a fun cupcake, add half the batter, some jam, rest of batter and bake/ice the same way as the cake. For Xmas I plan to try this!! Maybe add some food colouring to the coconut for a festive touch. Bake in red and green paper cups.

I want to rave about the filling!  Everyone loved it, definitely five stars!!  I added about 1/4 tsp. of fresh nutmeg for flavor and an extra tbs. of "juice" to get a consistency I liked.  The cake would be fine for cupcakes but for a layer cake though, mine wasn't as light or tender and didn't rise as high as other reviewers indicated.  Next time I might use cake flour or possibly a white cake mix adding coconut and almond extract.  The frosting sounds yummy but I made a variation of a 7 Minute Frosting sprinkled with toasted coconut to limit the calories from fat.  Thanks for posting! I would never have thought of combining a coconut cake with an apricot filling; an excellent combination of flavors!

A friend of mine asked for a coconut cake for her birthday, and as she happens to be a fan of the "Cat Who..." series, I figured this was a no-brainer! Bear in mind that I'm not a fan of coconut cakes myself, and yet I thought this was really good. Two main reasons: the hint of almond in the cake is delicious, and the tangy apricot filling is a great counterpoint to the sweetness of the cream cheese icing. For my own sake, I toasted the coconut that went into the batter (I generally like toasted coconut much better), and I thought it lent a nice flavor to the cakes themselves. I left the coconut on top plain, though... couldn't bear to mess with the classic snow-white "coconut cake" image. My friends kept saying "mmm" as they were eating it, so I'd say that was a thumbs-up!

This cake is marvelous - what a wonderful texture and heft!  This is not your average wimpy coconut cake...  
I followed the directions for the cake exactly, even setting the timer so I mixed the batter correctly.  I did not use an apricot filling, but used a lemon filling that I also got from Recipezaar.  I made my own whipped icing and toasted the coconut to sprinkle on the top and sides.  I will make this cake again and again!

I have made this cake for several special occassions now and my husband claims it as his favorite cake!!  He loves the coconut in the cake batter rather than as an accessory on top of it!  The almond extract accentuates the coconut flavor very nicely.  The apricot filling is delicious.  I usually lighten up the icing with a butter frosting or a whipped cream frosting and add toasted coconut to the top and sides.  The thing everyone enjoys about this cake is that it tastes like a good dense homemade cake and it tastes like you had a piece of cake!!!!

I was so excited when I came across this recipe. Whenever I see this on a menu I always order it, but now I can make this at home!!! I served it at our last party and it was a big hit! I added the juice of half a lemon to the recipe and used canned crab meat. This repice is delish!!!!

I love these biscuits...!!! They are so easy to make with only 5 ingredients and the combination of flavors is awesone with olive oil, chili powder, green chiles, Monterey jack cheeese, and I also added one chopped garlic clove...delicious. Thanks for sharing this recipe, Chef Koechin.

I made these as part of my Christmas Cookie Tray.
They are very
soft and chewy.  My kitchen smelled so good from the spices, while baking!
I will make these again!

Reduced the sugar by a third (still used full amount of molasses) and it was still pretty sweet! Had to be very careful as to the time in the oven. The cookies flattened nicely and you can barely lift them off the baking paper as they were still soft while hot. Mine took about 12 minutes in a 175 degrees C oven. Love the crispy on the outside and chewy on the inside texture and subtly spicy taste. Thanks, FarahC!

These were yummy! Chewy inside and crisp around the edges. I didn't even bother to roll them in sugar. I used my cookie scoop (every baker needs one) and sprinkled sugar on top before I baked them. This is the perfect Xmas cookie. Thanks!
very good-a keeper recipe for sure, thanks!!

Great cookies.  I think I would add a 1/4 cup more molasses next time and decrease the white sugar.  I made them for Thanksgiving - the kitchen was warm from all the holiday cooking and the cookies spread quite a bit in the oven so next time I think I will chill the dough before baking.  Also I didn't need the full 1/2 cup of sugar for rolling the cookies - start with a 1/4 cup and add as needed.

These cookies were simply amazing. Perfect in every way. I would like to note though, that instead of using 3/4 cup of shortening, I used 6 tbsp of shortening and 6 tbsp of lard.

Anyhow, thank you! I'll definitely make these again.
This was great! I took it to my youth group at my church and they adored it! thank you for posting this wonderful recipe.

This is rich and creamy and tasty.  I did as a previous poster suggested and just added the dry coffee to the pudding mix instead of disolving it first.  Works fine.  DS has already requested that I make this again, and we still have half of it left.

This recipe makes the perfect cookie!  I discovered it in time for my children's school Halloween party and have made the cookies four times over the past month.  The teachers confiscated the cookies at the Halloween party and kept them at the adult table; they said the cookies were too good to share with the kids.  My favorite batch includes 16 snack-size Mounds bars, 2 full-size Snickers with almonds, and dark chocolate covered raisins.  That batch was pretty and delicious.  It was even better the second day; the cookies stayed chewy and wonderful.  I added the salt in when I creamed the butter and sugar; I guess that was right.  I also made a batch without oatmeal and used all white sugar, butter flavor Crisco instead of butter, and chopped almonds instead of peanuts or pecans that time...fabulous!  No matter what way I make these, people rave about them and beg for the recipe.  Even the men at my office, not normally big fans of dessert, ask for these cookies.  I love chewy cookies, and this is my new standby recipe.
Great lemonade. Not too sweet. Loved the added cranberry flavor. Very refreshing on a hot day :)
I'm always looking for something new to make to drink during all this hot weather, and this quenched my thirst!

This is Excellent!
Served with rice and meal was really enjoyed.  Husband didn't like the idea of using thighs but later remarked how they worked -out well.  Will make again and serve with a chudney also.

I'm sorry, but we did not like this recipe. The chicken tasted mostly of peanut butter and otherwise did not have much flavor. Maybe I should have used Hot salsa instead of Medium? I can see myself putting this on an Asian salad or as chicken for some other recipe...

I first made these for Christmas and they were so popular I make them regularly throughout the year. They are the right combination of sweet and tart with a lovely vanilla finish. Quick to make and the recipe is very forgiving - You can pull the tart shells out of the freezer, toss in frozen cranberries, mix up the coconut mixture in a minute and bake.
Sounds yummy, can't wait to give this one a try!

I was hoping someone had posted this recipe.  I made these cookies the night this issue of Relish appeared in my paper because they looked so easy.  And they were.  I thought I had nuts, but I didn't, so I made them without, but I have macadamias in the cupboard now, waiting for the next batch.  I omitted the salt.  They were so, so, so good - you'd think with all this sugar that they'd be cloying, but they weren't.  Just delicious.  And did I mention easy?  I did need to cook them for the full 19 minutes, but I wouldn't have wanted to do longer.  I made 10 larger cookies instead of 15.

I tried it and it tastes great ... but they stuck to my paper and I buttered it as well.  I won't make them again unless I can figure out the sticking problem -- but the taste was good.  Thanks for posting.

Made these for dinner a couple of days ago.  I wanted to use up the tomatoes from our garden before they could get the chance to go bad.  This was a very easy recipe, and my family just loved it.  It sure beats trying to can tomatoes in 100-degree weather!

My husband and I both agree...5 Stars! This recipe is a 'keeper'...its delicious and easy to make.
The bacon really makes this dish outstanding.
One day I 'cheated' and added a small can of Rotel.  :) Yummy!
Thanks, LuckyMomof3 for sharing your recipe.

I wasn't impressed by this recipe. I felt adding the cake mix while the ice-cream was processing gave the mixture a gritty taste. (I tasted it a lot through the process.) I thought I added to much cake mix, because the flavor was very pronounced, but after it sat in the freezer, I couldn't even tell it was cake batter ice-cream. The ice-cream seemed to lose its flavor. I did enjoy the clumps I added after it was done freezing in the machince. It gave it the cake batter taste when I had a piece. I will try the cake mix in a different recipe by adding it before it goes into the machice so I can mix it in well. I like a smooth, not hard ice-cream. If you want an ice-cream that after freezing doesn't get rock solid, this recipe isn't for you. Maybe try, your favorite ice-cream recipe and try adding the mixture to it. I will not rate this recipe, because I feel it's my own personal opinion and others might not mind the things I do. All in all, it's not a bad ice-cream. Thanks for posting it! I gave it a shot!
It did have a cake batter flavor, but it also had a custard flavor which I'm not crazy about
This was DELICIOUS and very easy. I had lemon olive oil and I added a little fresh lemon juice and topped it with fresh ground pepper - Thanks!!

My hubby & I made these last yr & we fried them. They were so good. We plan on making them this week.  We use the Chi Chi brand cheese sauce on them/ (at the grocery) My hubby also uses green sauce sometimes.
This is a great recipe!  I usually use peanut butter cookie dough when I make mine!  They are yummy!!!

Excellent cookies, my kids had lots of fun helpng me make these.Your directions were very clear, but we had trouble figuring out just how much was too much or too little in the muffin tins. We used festive paper liners for the tins, and even though the cookies were not perfect, they still looked nice. We used Lennie's Perfect Peanut Butter Cookie dough #73960, a winning combo with your recipe. Thanks for sharing, Bethany!
LOVE THEM!!!!!!!!!!  used my own homemade peanut butter cookie dough though.  Mu husband loves them---------will be making again and again! Also very easy to make!

These cookies are fabulous.  I used peanut butter cookie dough instead of the sugar cookie dough and sprinkled mini holly candies on top of the melted chocolate.  They taste so good and look so festive.  They really look like little flowers.  Thanks so much for a great recipe.

I used a tube of Pillsbury Peanut Butter Cookies and these couldn't have been easier. It is true that you need to experiement with the thickness of the dough. Our first batch was too big, but with a little practice we got them just the right size. Once we put a ball of cookie dough in the pan we used a melon baller to put in a hole and mush it up the sides of the tin. This method worked the best. This was an great,easy recipe to use with the grandchildren.

Yummers!

Never made grits before but these turned out creamy and delicious:)  

I sauteed in a small red onion and finished with grated cheese and pepper.  Will be making this again, tx for sharing!

When I made this recipe, it turned out to be a very dense, heavy bread.  I really liked the flavor that the molasses gave it but the texture was off for me.  I don't know if wheat gluten would make a difference?  Thanks, though.

Wonderful flavor!  I baked mine at 335F for 38 min; it turned out quite light and fluffy.  The dough was somewhat wet when I took it out of the bread machine; it sprawled out on the baking sheet more than normal since I baked it in a single round loaf.  (Next time I'll do 2 loaves instead.)  I did substitute cornmeal for half of the ground flax seed, and I used exclusively white flour since I'd run out of ww.  I was looking for a recipe with both oats and flax seed, and this is a winner!  Thanks for posting :)
This dressing is marvelous. It is sweet and tangy!
My family loves this pie! I have made it twice now, and plan to make it again over the holidays. The graham cracker crust tastes wonderful with the vanilla. Thanks for posting, Roxygirl!
These are really good. I season with salt and pepper and drizzle a little honey over them before baking.

Yum!  This recipe provides just the right ratio of molasses:sugar.  We just wanted this as a topping instead of making popcorn balls, so we decreased the amount of sugar and molasses by half, and added a small pinch of salt over the bowl of popcorn before eating.  Thanks for sharing!
This is sooo delicious! I added in some bacon bits and served with my stuffed chicken and WOW it was so tasty. I will be making this alot in the future! Thank you for posting this!! A winner!
Any recipe that the worlds pickiest eaters will eat- gets 5 stars from me!! This meal was delicious & easy to make! This is a kid pleaser! The whole family loved it! Thank you!

I have been frying chicken in my oven for years this way but I would use a whole stick of butter in place of the shortening and butter.  Really, really liked the idea of the biscuits with the chicken drippiings and me and my husband enjoyed.  It is truly the best way to fry chicken.  Note:  I only cooked my chicken 20 minutes instead of the 35 and then another 15 for biscuits.  My husband always tell me I cook meats too long and they are dry..by cooking it only 20 minutes initially the result was perfection.  Thanks.
This is a terrific recipe really easy and delish. I bought the bisquik just to try this recipe and we loved it ! Thanks Nana Lee!

I tried this tonight for dinner and it was great. I was in the mood for comfort food...and this was just what I needed. I used Darling76's Missouri Mix (Missouri Mix) which I really recommend. It made the meal! I just added enough milk to the mix to make a nice lumpy biscuit and they were so good with all of the juices from the pan. We will be doing this again! Thanks!
The chicken was wonderful; we didn't care for the biscuits that much.  I'll keep doing the chicken this way, though.
Turned out really good!!  We really enjoyed it!

I made this on the weekend, and, while I was intending on using the crockpot, got tied up and ended up just making it on the stove-top. Next time (and there will be a next time!) I will try the crockpot. At any rate, I really loved this - it was the first time I have made anything with sweet potatoes, and I really liked their flavour and texture in this. I did add some cumin to this (I never measure - probably 2 tsp), plus about a tsp of cinnamon. Thanks for posting this!

This was delicious and easy. I subbed half the beef broth with red wine and followed the rest to a T. As someone else suggested, I think I will throw in some frozen peas next time I make this. It seems not all crockpots are created equal, mine was done in 5 hours on low.

Started a diet about 6 months ago, and I missed having good homemade eggnog at the holidays.  The problem with the recipe is that it tastes like liquid vanilla pudding, not eggnog.  I gave it 3 stars because I realize that there are limited sugar free options out there.  If you like the store bought light eggnog, but want less sugar and more vanilla, then this would be a good choice.  Or, if you really love vanilla pudding, and want it in its liquid form, then this is definitely your recipe.
Loved this!  I think the mustard gives it an unexpected but wonderful dimension!

This is the martini that inspired my martini glass collection!  
I love Bombay Sapphire!  There's a subtle licorice to it that I haven't experienced in any other gin.  This is my favorite way to have it also, with very little vermouth.  (p.s. I have that glass in my ever growing martini glass collection)

Yum!  I went out and bought vermouth just for this.  I did not use olives or capers but instead added a lemon twist. Very delicious!
Perhaps this will be my new signature drink, especially because we just got a set of martini glasses for our wedding.

I love your soup ~ and decided to make enough for an ARMY as usual! The taste is just right and the only different thing I used in it was Seasond Salt instead of regluar Salt ! I think you got it just right !!! Thank for this one!

I just ran across this recipe in my favorites file, and I can't believe I haven't rated it yet.  To date, this has been one of my most frequently served Zaar soups.  This is a wonderful soup, and it lends itself well to variations depending on the cheese you may have on hand.  It is also a natural for crock pot cooking, so that's another big plus!
Thanks for the recipe, Highland Lass.  It has become a family favorite.

This is really easy to make.  I like the flavours, particularly the dijon, although I only added 1 tablespoon of the dijon as I wanted to see what that would taste like before I considered adding more.  I think I'll leave it at the 1 tbsp as that flavour level works for me.  I also used skim milk (1/2 a cup) instead of the cream or evaporated milk, and it worked well.  How much milk you use would depend on how thick you wanted it.  The soup was tasty and I'll make it again!
Delicious!!  The only change I made was to add a few broccoli florets because the cauliflower I had was very small.  We enjoyed this and I am looking forward to leftovers.  Thanks!

This was very tasty.  The polenta was a little soggy, but did not take away from the taste of the veggies. I had to bake much longer than one hour before the veggies were tender.  I suggest topping with cheese the last 20 minutes or so.  Very good left-over dish.  I will be making this again.
This drink is delicious! It's so fruity and tropical! I agree with Lennie... who needs the rum! I'll be making this again often in the summertime. Thanks for sharing a great recipe, chef FiFi.
This was ok.  I enjoyed the pinaple and coconut flavor bit it felt like something was missing.  I think next time I'll add a tablespoon of vanilla extract and a maraschino cherry.

I have made this many times, and I'm just now writing a review...it is DELICIOUS! This is my go-to leftover chicken dish. I make it with pastured butter and homemade chicken stock for added nutrition. A family favorite for sure!
Awesome!  thank you!

I made this for a dinner party and it was a really big hit (once people got up enough nerve to try it).  I might add less green pepper next time because several of the guests commented that they really enjoyed it except for the green pepper.  My husband who is on a raw food diet loved it.  It was quick easy and provided a really nice exotic touch.  I'll be making it again.
This is my favorite way to make spanakorizo (spinach and rice), and is  delicious served with a bit of feta cheese on the side.

These are absolutely delicious. They'd run out of white chocolate in the shop so I used milk chocolate instead. I halved the recipe and got 12 out of it but they still all cooked in 10 minutes. They're gorgeous whilst still warm from the oven. I used both walnuts and pecans. Thanks!

This is soooooo good. Thankyou and you're absolutely right it tastes like a recipe I tried from a Thai restaurant. I have been searching through many and this is the best to date!! I reduced the amount of Red curry paste to 1& 1/4 tsp and that was hot enough for us. Thankyou very much!!!

Oh my KittenCal, you've outdone yourself with this one.  We had it for dinner last night with Basamati rice and Broccoli with Sesame Oil. YUMMMM!!  The only thing is that it didn't say when to add the chopped red pepper so I put it in with the garlic and onions and it turned out fine so I suppose that's whatyou had in mind.  Thanks for posting = we all loved it!!!!

I made this last night and took the chance of testing on friends, which I donÂ´t normally do!! It was an absolute winner! Mango chutney with Thai curry? Who would have thought it!! I had just used the last of the Red curry paste and took the chance of using Masaman. Had no red peppers in so used Spanish frying peppers. The rest was the same (I used 1 400ml can of milk) and my God, what a flavor! Thanks for this, a masterpiece.
Scrumptious is Right, Wonderful Flavor & very easy!  Served with potato salad and deviled eggs.  Excellent summer meal!

Wonderful wings, easy to prepare with on-hand ingredients.  It was to hot to grill outside so I used my indoor George Foreman type grill opened flat so I had more grilling space. The wings were rather large and it did take almost an hour to cook on that grill.  I followed the recipe exactly but then I used a Vermont Honey Mustard salad dressing for a dipping sauce.  No complaints from any of the picky eaters in my house.  This recipe is a keeper!

These were really good.  When I first mixed the marinade together, I thought the mustard flavor was going to be to strong for my kids liking, but after I marinaded the wings for 6 hours and then cooked on the bbq for 30 minutes, the flavors blended nicely together.  Everyone said they liked them.

These were fabulous.  I used the Ron Popiel roterriere and they were wonderful...not a wing left with just three of us.  I will definitely make this again.  The mustard disapetes and the flavor is incredible.  Wow...Wow....thanks for the wonderful recipe!
I marinated these overnight, and baked in 350 oven for about 45 minutes. They were very good. The mustard wasn't overpowering.
Way too salty... couldnt eat these at all. followed all directions. :(
These were nice. Definately nicer the longer you marinate. They were still nice for lunch the next day. thanks for recipe

What a wonderful recipe!  I made it as an appetizer on Memorial Day and BOY before I could get a second piece, it was gone. We will be having it again this week.  Thanks for the recipe.  I would not change a thing.
Wow, this deserves more than 5 stars.  Wonderful flavor even though I forgot the olive oil.  Went thru 4 lbs. of fresh wings in very little time.  Thank you for sharing such a great recipe!

Everyone here RAVED over this chicken. I used drumsticks (10 of them), & Jack Daniels Hickory Smoked mustard, minced garlic from a jar (about 1 T.), marinated 4-5 hours,  and grilled about 20 min. each side. AWESOME! Definitely will make again...

Wonderful !!!!!!!!! 

I made them last weekend and we all loved them .
Ri :)

I didn't have any chicken wings but after reading the reviews I wanted to try the marinade so I did it on chunks of boneless chicken breast and instead of grilling (I got home too late to wait for charcoal!) I cooked them on the stovetop in a non stick pan and OMG! This is soooo savory! I can't wait to try on chicken wings. I was worried about the amount of mustard but it was perfect, not overwhelming just added a nice flavor. The soy gave it so much more flavor than I could have imagined. The drippings in the pan made a wonderful savory gravy too, poured it over rice. This is a keeper! Thank you!

great flavor. Everybody loves it for tonight BBQ.  I used wings and breast both on the grill. The wings seem to taste better, very moist. I love it. I think I will do it again in our family BBQ next time.
I am rating it a four...only because they were gone so fast I didn't get to try one! im assuming they were good :)

Made this tonight for 20 sailing instructors for a dinner ages 16 - 20.  Also served ribs, homemade bread & 2 pasta dishes.  I marinated the wings overnight & made as directed.  "Kids" came back for seconds for the pasta, ribs & bread.  No more takers for the wings.  Also made 2 homemade desserts ~ brownies & cupcakes.  The only leftovers was 1/2 the wings & 1 rib.  I saved the rib but thru the wings out.  Will not make these wings again.  Not a hit.

I won't give this any stars, because I messed up the recipe.  I had copied two recipes on a sheet of paper, and I ended up adding 1 tablespoon of brown sugar instead of the ground ginger.  I also didn't have Dijon mustard, I just used regular prepared mustard.  However, they did come out great and everyone loved them.  I marinated them for a few hours and did them on the bbq.  I'm looking forward to the leftovers for lunch today.
This was excellent.  We made the recipe exactly as listed and loved the tang of the mustard-based sauce.  Very tasty and definitely not overly mustard-y.  Good stuff!

I made these for our 4th of July BBQ today as I wanted some thing "different."  

We all really enjoyed them.  When I first made the marinade and tasted it, I though it was awful and feared that they would taste really bad but there were 6 of us and we all enjoyed them.

So delicious, so easy. I had my doubts about the primary mustard content of the marinade but when you taste them, there is only a hint of mustard. They are just simply delicious.
The other thing I liked about them is there was no sugar, other than what is contained in the soy sauce. So, diabetics, like my cousin, can enjoy them knowing they're not loaded with sugar. Thanks for posting this recipe!!

This really was exceptional!  I marinated wings and chicken breasts for 24 hours and grilled on the BBQ.   Yummo!   Using the breasts sliced up on a salad for lunch the next day.  This will most certainly be on the "best" list for marinades.  Mahalo nui for the recipe!
This was so yummy.  I think I added too much flour because it kind of came out gravy-ish but it was still good!
Very nice!  Not too sweet, not too tart, and I like the herbal note contributed by the rosemary.  it seemed to be a hit with the fam, not much left over after tgiving dinner.
What a way to start the day. Perfect for overnight guests.

I made these today as a Halloween surprise for approximately 150 kids. I can't tell you how many times I heard, "Are those REALLY worms?!?" My reply to that was a grin and, "We serve only the FINEST worms!" :D I made sure to use the ketchup, which indeed upped the "gross factor". The kids thought it was pretty cool. :D

I made these for a catered Halloween party for 40 kids last night. I used little yeast rolls, and tossed with bbq sauce instead of catsup. 

I also took black sesame seeds and put a few "eyes" on some of the worms....it was truly grotesque!! The kids went wild when i put them on the buffet.

I served this last night and it was a big hit with the young and young at heart.  Also served witchy-poo fingers, devided eyeball eggs, creepy body bits dip, human eyeballs, and penne spinach bake.  All were great and made for a fun pre-trick or treat party.
I made these for my girls today and they loved them. I added some musterd with the ketchup to make it even yuckier looking.

This is a great idea not only for Halloween but any time of the year if you should have hotdogs on hand and no hotdog rolls in the bread box.  I have used this same method when I've been out of rolls but would love a hotdog for lunch.  I butter one side of two slices of bread and toast the buttered side in a skillet.  Place the worms on the untoasted side of one slice, add  the 'worms', desired hotdog condiments, cover with the other slice untoasted side down.  Works great..!!  Thanks for sharing stimied.
Made these for my two (8 & 3) very grumpy boys for lunch and by the first bite they were happy and giggling.  Thanks for a super cute take on an ordinary lunch, they loved it!
I made these for Halloween, and they were great. Such a cute idea!
Very cute- I made these for our Halloween Party and the kids loved them!
I made these for my kids before they went trick or treating and they loved it! At the time they were 13,12, and 8.  Just might be a tradition at my house! Thanks for a great idea!
I made this for the kids, and they loved them. We watched How to eat Fried Worms the night before, so I had to do some convincing that it really wasn't worms.
I made this for Halloween! To make it more wormy, I added sauteed onions...as you can see in my photo! I used BBQ sauce instead of ketchup! This was a great Halloween dinner!
This was absolutely delicious!! When I saw the ingredients I just couldn't pass up making this, and I'm so glad that I tried it (so was my family). Thank you for such a tasty recipe!

Reminds me of the bolognese entres which I love dearly.  Very tasty dish, I too used different pasta, and made a few changes per personal preferences, but really a great dish to through together on a weeknight.

5 stars for something quick and easy to make with things you most likely have on hand and tastes really good. You can't beat that! The only thing I did different was saute the onions and some garlic while browning the meat, I also used italian seasoning and added a bit more sauce. Thanks for posting!
Great family recipe.  I made it for dinner tonight and the husband and kids loved it.  Easy to make and very filling.  Thanks for a wonderful recipe.

Very very good.  With just minor seasoning changes.  I had run across a very similar recipe years and years ago, and our family used to refer to it as poor man's lasagne.  I bake it in two 9" square pans and freeze one.  Ty for posting and bringing it back to my rotation.

I made this using ground turkey and increased the spices a bit to suit our tastes and it came out wonderfully. If you're crazing something like lasagna without all the work, this is a good substitute and will certainly be something we make often as the cool weather rolls in.
I've made this several times and everyone loves it.  I don't layer anything - I just mix it all together and bake.  It's wonderful.  Can't go wrong with this one!

We really like this recipe! I did not have wide egg noodles so  I used medium shells and added some mozzarella in with the cheddar. Everything else was done just as written. We had for dinner last night, lunch today, and dinner again tonight so you know we like it!
Not much taste and hard to make into a ball - too sticky.

I really did not need to submit a review because this is the recipe I have been using for years, almost 40 years to be exact. The title says it all, Best Cherry Pie. Thanks for posting Luby Luby Luby.

Absolutely delicious! Not too sweet or tart. I did adjust a few things: I left out the red food coloring and cut back the sugar to 1 cup. I also added 1/4 tsp of vanilla in addition to the almond flavoring and gave it a big dash of cinnamon.  Also cut the lemon juice in half. I'm sure it is excellent as is, but I just like to improvise a little. Be sure and have a cookie sheet underneath because this likes to bubble over!
I left the recipe as is and really enjoyed the almond extract.  It gave it a little something extra.  This is the best cherry pie filling I've ever tasted!
All my Thanksgiving guests & I agree...this is AWESOME!!! This is a keeper!

This is without a doubt the Best Cherry Pie I have ever eaten in my 51 years on this Earth.  I cut the sugar down to 1 cup and left everything else the same.  People, you MUST try this pie.  Thanks for sharing this Luby.

This was my first attempt at a "real" cherry pie (I've always used canned pie filling before).  Everyone thought it was wonderful. WIll keep this one and make again!  I even did the lattice top and it looked fanastic! Thanks for submitting!

Great cherry pie! I left out the food coloring, but otherwise made it just as is. I also used the pie crust recipe from Illinois Apple Pie (#8423), which is simply the best pie crust recipe ever. This pie was just right for us - not too tart, but not too sickly sweet either.

This the second time I have made this recipe. Both times recipe AS IS! It was so yummy when I made it just for DH and myself, this time I am taking it to share with friends at dinner tonight. Not only the BEST it is PERFECT!

This was my first time trying to make my own cherry pie.  My husband and I thought that it was very good.  I personally thought that the filling was a little dry - when I make this again (and this is the recipe I will be using) I will add a little more of the cherry juice.  Very easy to make - a definate keeper.

This pie is really really good!  I got a lot of compliments on it! The filling is not too sweet, not too tart.  Very good!  Will definitely make again!  Next time, I will put in another 1/4 cup cherry juice and slightly more lemon juice, maybe another 3/4 of a tsp just so the filling is more moist. But that is just to suit our tastes! I made this with the store ready pie crust and it was Outstanding! Thank you for sharing the recipe Luby!

Great recipe-- super easy! I added an extra 1 tsp of cornstarch since some cooks commented that it turned out kind of runny. The consistency was perfect. Everything else followed the recipe to a T. Lots of compliments, definitely a repeater!

This was the first time I made a cherry pie and it was delicious!  The filling spilled over the sides a little bit so it didn't look very pretty, but it tasted just as good (probably better) than any other cherry pies I've had before.  You could probably even substitute some/all of the sugar with Splenda for a lighter alternative.

DH did not like this at all and I have seen him polish off a cherry pie in two days. Says it is too watery and made the bottom crust soggy. Even though I only used about 1/8 tsp of almond extract we felt it was too strong. I used my own pie crust recipe which I have used forever so I am sure it wasn't the crust. Recipe #300887. I am not a cherry pie lover so I guess this one is an expensive mistake. So sorry.

OMGosh!! What a delicious pie, and I made it! How impressive this pie is . . thanks to Luby. I used 1 cup Splenda (husband is diabetic) and 1/4 cup sugar, no red food coloring, and used vanilla vs. almond extract (no likey the almond). No problem with runny juices, pie was total perfection! I purchased an awesome cherry/olive pitter (OXO brand) for approximately five/six cups of fresh cherries. THANK YOU for making me look good, and feel good about my first homemade "cherry" pie!

A good review is nothing new for this recipe, but I wanted to express my appreciation.  I was asked to make a cherry pie for Thanksgiving, and having never made one, I looked to recipezaar for guidance.  Because I was making a 10" pie, instead of an 8 or 9" one, I increased the filling amount, using 3 cans of cherries and increasing the other ingredients by 50%.  I held the almond extract to a scant 1/4 tsp and added the same amount of vanilla extract.  Bonus:  the one cup of filling that wouldn't fit in the pie shell baked nicely in its own little dish, so I could sample the completed, baked filling without cutting into the pie.  The pie was a big hit yesterday, so this recipe will go in my Thanksgiving recipe book for future years.    Thanks to Luby Luby Luby for sharing the recipe, and to all the reviewers who encouraged me to give it a try.
I made this for my sons and they loved it! Only thing they said was that it could have been just a tiny bit thicker..that could have been my error tho.
I've made this a few times, but haven't posted a rating until now.  My dad loved this recipe. It reminded him of the cherry pie my grandmother used to make. Thanks for posting...very yummy!

So glad you posted this recipe--I made this over the weekend for my crew of hungry farmers who were planting corn--they loved it! Definitely a keeper! Can't wait to make it with the cherries from our cherry trees!

Oh my gosh, your recipe is amazing!!  This was the first time I've ever made a cherry pie, so I was really pleased that it turned out so well!!  I didn't change a thing... it made a devilishly good "Vam-Pie" for my Halloween party :)  I'm gonna go eat another slice right now!!

Great tasting pie!  Do want to warn anyone who tries to make this.  I made it EXACTLY as directed and when I went to check on it around 50 mins it was very liquidy!  I was freaking out thinking the pie was ruined but it was not!  I covered the perimeter crust with foil and baked it for 15 mins longer.  The filling was still very loose when I took it out but after it sat and cooled for a bit it firmed up right away.  It is a very good cherry pie- more tart than sweet (like you would get from a cherry pie filling out of a can).  I will definitely make it again.

This was my first attempt at making a cherry pie and it turned out great!  I also brushed egg wash on crust before baking.  I made this for my hubbies 50th birthday and he loved it, (with vanilla ice cream).  Mine was not liquidy at all.  Thanks for an easy delicious recipe

Well here comes ANOTHER 5 star rating for this cherry pie!!!  I made this especially for my daughter in law who requested a cherry pie for thanksgiving this last week.  I've made alot of pies in my time, but my cherry pie recipe was just ok, so when I read the reviews for this one i thought. . .ok, I'll try this once and BOY HOWDY am I glad I did!!  Rave reviews from all my family members, even the ones who were just ok about cherry pie!!  Thanks for posting this delicious cherry pie recipe Luby Luby Luby, definately a keeper. :0)

This is the first cherry pie I have ever made, and it was really good! I used vanilla extract instead of almond, since I don't have almond extract; but, otherwise, I followed the recipe exactly. Next time I will use 1 can of tart cherries and 1 can of regular cherries because it was a little tart for my husband. I might also add a little more (or double) cornstarch next time, too. But still a great recipe.

I wish I could give this extra stars!  It's delicious.  And it is very pleasing to the kiddos.  Next time I make a meal for just the hubby and me, I might add some green onion to the mix.  This is awesome.  I highly recommend!
Our family just loves this recipe.  We have also substituted pork for the chicken and it has turned out very good also!  Thank you for sharing this recipe:)

This was okay...but I was surprised by the lack of depth of flavor with the ingredients used.  My only change was to used dried ginger in place of fresh (didn't have fresh on hand). Otherwise, followed it exactly.  Sorry I couldn't give this a higher rating, but appreciate you posting it.  I'll keep searching for a teriyaki recipe that suits our family's tastes.
It was ok, but there was way too much garlic!! Will probably make again but cut down on the garlic and ginger.

A little too much ginger for my taste.  I think if you cut it back to 2 tbs. it would be perfect.  This was a good dish just not very teriyaki tasting.  The ginger was overpowering.  Thank you for the recipe.  I will use again with my adjustments.

hey a new family fav!  thank you!  I used apricot preserves this tm, however, I plan to prepare this dish each tm w/every selection of sauce offered up. the family is REALLY looking fwd to the plum sauce (found in the Asian isle of the supermarket). I made a few changes due to availability:  ground ginger vs fresh, coconut oil vs veggie, low-sodium soy sauce vs the everyday & 4 tsp of bottled minced garlic vs fresh garlic. thanx for sharing!
This was just ok-- needs more flavor. Next time I'm cooking the whole thing on the grill. I'll use less oil and more soy sauce. I'll add ginger and orange juice or some grated orange rind.
I loved this recipe. It was easy to prepare and tasty. I did use fresh onion, fresh garlic and sea salt but otherwise followed the instructions. I will be making this again. Thank you for sharing:)

I thought these were really great, and  I am not a big cannoli fan.  My husband thought they were good too.  The only thing I would do different is not fill them until they are ready to be served.  They did get soggy pretty quickly.  The taste of the filling is superb.  I will definintely use this again.

These are the bomb!!! We made them for our desert on a camping trip for a Italian Night theme meal. They were devoured in minutes. So very easy and I made the filling during the day when I was bored. Thanks for the great recipe.

I printed this recipe November 22, 2003, and have made it so many times since then Iv'e lost count.  This recipe takes a little time but so, so worth it.  Crunchy bread, creamy cheese, sweet strawberries and a crunch sweet creme brulee type topping-MMMMMM.  Thank you so much for a divine recipe s'kat!

I love these! Just made them again last night- ds requested them. :-) I never let the sausage cool before adding the cheese, so it's a bit messy stuffing the pitas, but oh, well. Really good and so easy for those nights when I really don't feel like cooking!

Very tasty.  It was easy to make.  I used marinara sauce (#39629)
I'll make it again. Thanks.
The best hummus I've tasted!! Thanks!!
Super recipe. I made 1/2 the amount, added 2 large garlic cloves as we love garlic & it came out perfect!!thanks

I made this today. I have always wondered what Hummus was. Then I started hearing alot about it on health food shows that I enjoy watching. So I decided to give it a whirl. I enjoyed it and was surprized at how good it tasted.A girlfriend of mine said it was like she was eating something sinful. YEAH SINFULLY DELISH!!! The tahini ws a little much for me. I will definatly be cutting that amount down. I also put in cumin and more garlic. I left out the paprika but I think I will try putting some in next time. Over all it was great thanks for sharing.
Great Recipe! I love hummus and hate paying so much for it in the grocery store. This is the firs time that I have made it at home and this was a home run!

I loved all the veggies in this chili recipe!  Next time I'll chop the carrots smaller so they are more disguised - my husband didn't think they belonged in chili.  I did cook the ground beef first before adding all the other ingredients - wasn't sure if I had to do that, the directions didn't say.  This turned out great - a do over!  Thanks, Lottidawe!
Unusual but very good.

Made for Spring 2010 PAC
This was a really good dessert.  I love Boston Cream "Pie", and it is hard to get around here.  This was an excellent substitute.  I didn't think it would be and I was very pleasently surprised.  However, it isn't cheap to make, the small Sarah Lee frozen Pound Cake was $4.50.  Nontheless, it uses ingredients that you could keep on hand and have Boston Cream Pie/Cake anytime you want.  That is priceless to me.
Very different and delightful! Thanks for the recipe!
Looks good, tastes good, and healthy to boot.
This recipe was very tasty and i will definately use it on my vegitarian friends. Next time I won't use as much sugar as it was a personally a little sweet.

I wanted to do a "trial run" before serving these at a party.  Followed the recipe exactly.  We enjoyed these very much, but next time I will increase the ketchup to 1 cup because we like a more pronounced "ketchupy" taste.  Also, we liked these minis warm, so I will put them on a warming tray at the party.  Thank you for a great addition to my appetizer collection.

These were pretty good. I can't put my finger on what's missing in the flavor equation for me, hence comments without a rating. They are definitely better when warm. Thanks for posting a clever appetizer. I love to try new things.

pretty good, a little denser and greasier than what I thought they would be but still very good.  I love that you can make them and freeze them.  Halfway through I added some bell pepper and a lot of DILL pickle relish and I think those made good additions to the batter.

My boys really enjoyed this.  I made it in a pie plate, instead of making mini muffins, as my muffin tin was being used for Halloween treats.  The boys have asked for this to be made again, so into the rotation it goes!
I made a few changes, but overall stuck close to the original. Fantastic!!
These were just ok for us. We served them at our Christmas party, and they bombed. They were just bland- nothing special. Sorry. I wanted to like them :(

Really good. Go ahead and use a whole pound of meat. I had cooked a pound of ground turkey and put half of it in the recipe, but didn't know what to do with the other half. I just dumped the rest in. Next time I will either add a little more ketchup to the mix, or dollop a little on the tops. Husband said I could make these again so they must be good.

This is a really great recipe, my family enjoyed it. However the next time that I make it I will be serving it hot, fresh out of the oven (maybe ala mode). I sampled it both hot and after refrigerating and it's much better warm!! I will be making it again, it was super! Tell grandma thanks Starrynews!
This is awesome! The taste is soo yummy! Thanks alot for this. I sometimes use ground meat with this wich is also nice..

Ok I made some changes to this to make it low-cal. I've been dieting and have been craving some variety. WOW! It was sensational. I used fat free cream cheese, used 3 laughing cow wedges to replace the montery jack, and used no calorie Pam Olive Oil spray. Instead of folding it in half - I topped it with a 2nd tortilla and cut them in 4. I made this into two meals of 236 calories each! Thanks!!!
Cool and unusual recipe.  My 3 year old loves it and my DH ate half of it in a couple days.  I barely got any.  Will make again for sure!

Very good! Both my boys loved it. i've made it twice in 2 weeks. The second time i made muffins which my son promptly ate about 6! Its not as heavy as pound cake in my opinion but still really good and is now my go to recipe for banana bread. thanks!
Very easy and yummy!  I was pleasantly surprised by the flavors and lightness of the recipe.  I agree with another reviewer in that this is not a pound cake, but more like a banana spice cake.
I didn't make the salmon filling given here, but did find many of the tips and techniques for preparation very helpful. I will try the salmon filling soon and review it. Thanks, Lynne.
Really good lemon bars. The best part to me was the crust: it didn't crumble apart very much like other recipes I've tried. My sons and I made these together, and we all loved them.

This Chili is not for the weak of heart or sensitive of taste buds!!! It was smokin' hot, but just how I like it! In fact, I loved the flavor. Unfortunately for the rest of my family, they had to wait a little longer until I got back from the store with more beans to thin out the spice some. I also served it over polenta and a small bed of rice to cool it off. If you have a family like mine, with mixed taste buds and spice tolerances, I'd start with a lot less chili powder (1 tsp) and a little less chipotle (2 chopped) for starters, then work your way up to the comfort level of your least spice tolerance in the household. Others can alway ADD chilis and spice to get it to their liking. I know what adjustments I need to make for next time, and there definitely will be a next time, the whole family absolutely loved the taste once I cooled it down some.

I made these for Christmas Day and we all concluded that they were very bland. I made 2 dozen in mini muffin tins and used about half the hash browns and all the filling. I had a note to add bacon but forgot:(

It was pretty good.  Everyone liked it, I did have a problem combining the pudding mix and milk, there just wasn't enough liquid and it turned rather crumbly and didn't fold into the cool whip very easily.  Not as much cheesecake flavor as I thought it would have.  But, overall it was still tasty.
I also made this lite and it was wonderful.  I will be making this when I want to make a special dessert.

Please do not use this recipe if you are going to can it... Using canners or hot water baths is no place for guessing games. It is very important to follow tested recipes as the interior of the jarred fruit or vegetable has to reach at least 155 for a proper period of time dependent on what you are cooking. Salt is also used in canning recipes as a preservative and does effect the temperature of the interior of the jar. OVER PROCESSING IS AS BAD AS UNDER PROCESSING!!! DO NOT DO IT

This is a fantastic recipe! Everyone in my family loved this moist, delicious cake. And it makes an awesome fall cake with the cinnamon and nutmeg. A winner!

UPDATE: I had a bumper crop of pears this year and I have made this cake FIVE times so far. I will probably make several more before the pears are all used up. I don't bother with the icing, the cake is absolutely perfect alone. I'm putting all the extras in my freezer for use over the holidays. I can't stress enough how wonderful this cake is.

We really enjoyed this recipe. I went a little light on the gochujang (I love the flavor but it's got a kick) and I only used about 2lbs of short ribs. I will definately make the full recipe next time. Thanks for posting.

This cake was so moist and flavourful...I loved the addition of the chocolate chips however I didn't have any pecans to add...I will definately be making this again...Thanks Michelle for passing on this tasty recipe...It's a keeper!
I rated this 4 stars yesterday but I just heated up a couple squares for breakfast(I know wierd) and I changed my mind.  These are a five.  Thank you!

This was very tasty!!! I made it according to recipe, except that I added an additional 1 1/2 fresh, blanched, chopped jalapeños that were leftover from making jalapeño poppers. It was a huge hit. At this point, I still have a couple of pieces left, and I'm inclined to hide them for my breakfast tomorrow!!! By the way, if you cut them into larger slices and serve it with side dishes, it makes an excellent meatless brunch/dinner. I might try varying this by adding some ham. Thanks for sharing an excellent recipe that I will be making again and again!

Wow, a cheese lover's DREAM!!! I sure enjoyed these as did the rest of my party guests. I had to give the recipe out to two people! That would be considered a hit! Very easy to make too which is nice. Thanks so much!
Kimberley, these are delicious.  I just made a batch and I can't stop eating them!  Thanks for sharing.

I requested these to be brought in on my birthday at work.  My friend made them as posted, these were good but even though I love cheese this was a bit too cheesy.  I know that it's made with all cheese so it'll be cheesy!  I will make these for sure with some minor changes, perhaps less cheese or cheese that isn't so oily when it gets melted.  The amount of jalapeno's were not spicy enough for me but that's easily fixed.  Good recipe to experiment on not to mention easy to make.

Very flavourful and moist cake, not too rich and that's how I like it. I reduced the cinnamon to just a pinch and the vanilla to one teaspoon. It came out perfectly, thank you for the recipe. My family loved it.

Oh my goodness!  I didn't give 5 stars just because the guilt factor of eating/making something sooooooo bad for me!!  I halved the recipe with good results.  I shook the shredded cheese in one BIG ziplock bag with the flour and the chopped up jalapenos, dumped that in a pan, then mixed the egg and milk in the bag with a fork.  No clean up!  Very tasty, a bit oily (but hey--it's a pound of cheese!).

I made this as a treat for my self on SuperBowl Sunday. I threw some veggies in there so I wouldn't feel so guilty lolol

This is a wonderful dish and I'm going to figure out how to make it less fattening but still as flavorful. Thanks!

Made this for a Memorial Day gathering and it was a hit with everyone! Couldn't believe how easy and quick it was to fix. Oh, I did substitute roasted chilies for the jalapeno's (personal preference)and instead of cheddar and jack cheese I used a blend of Mexican cheeses.  Thank you for the fantastic recipe. felines
A big hit at the party! It was a bit too fattening for me (but I don't eat things like chicken wings either.)People were fighting to finish the pan! Thanks for a great recipe I will make again.

These were good. I did like them more than DH.  I think the evaporated milk added a good flavor.  I loved the crunchy edges the most. DH didn't hate them but didn't rave about them either. I think he just ate them to make me happy.  I think i might make these again just for me.

Excellent!  Made for a tailgate - served room temp.   The leftovers, the few that we had, were great warmed up the next day for breakfast.  Only used 1 1/2 pounds cheese, one pound of which ws lowfat.
We had an Auburn/Alabama tailgate party at school. I brought this and everyone loved it! I am going to watch the game at a friend's house and this is what I am bringing.

These were very good and very easy.  I used a little less cheese and only used jalepenos on half.  I made these for a party and they went very quickly.  Not for calorie counters!  I cut these in to 1 inch squares which was a great bite.
Oh MAMA! These were so gooood and easy to make. Thanks !

Well, I must say we loved these so much! I did use more Jalapeno peppers since we really like hot stuff! These are so good! I really enjoyed this recipe and it was very easy to make.  I ate for breakfast the next day! Awesome!!!
Thanks so much for sharing!
