The roasted veggies looked and tasted great; they had lots of oil, and perhaps with half the dressing, the salad would have had a better mouth feel.  I'd halve the feta as well.

Couldn't stop tasting!   Every bite had new flavors.  Wow.   Changes I'd recommend, like others said, cut down on oil...I cut oil in half (both for roasted veg and the dressing)  and it was still plenty.  We preferred abt 4 oz of feta. I think 3/4 lb would overpower the dish for us.  And, zucchini is an excellent sub for eggplant, as someone else suggested.      It's a beautiful dish, some one needs to post a picture!
I had forgotten how delightful and simple dirt cake could be!  I used 1 chocolate pudding and 1 french vanilla.

This was very good. I didn't have evaporated milk so I used FF 1/2 and 1/2. I garnished with green onions. Sconehead, no offense but unless you killed your own turkey and ground it up and made your own cheddar cheese from cows you milk - you do in fact eat processed foods. Just because food isn't labeled "processed" doesn't mean it isn't. Nearly every thing Americans eat is processed in some way.

This was really, really tasty!  In my opinion, the wine is what makes this sauce, It smelled amazing while cooking and tasted even better.
Excellent recipe Lennie!

I only had bit over 1/2 pound of sole so adjusted the ingredients. Used Emeril's Seasoning instead of paprika. Also only had lime juice so substituted it.  We just finished it and both loved it.  The topping keeps the thin sole from drying out in the oven.  Was very easy to prepare.
Mmmm,everybody loved this one! The veggie mix is perfect and the dumplings go so well with it. Thanks for a new family favourite!
These were wonderful!!!  Very easy and my whole family (in-laws too) loved it!!!
Nice flavour combination.

Nothing better than grilled corn in the husk! I have made it this way many times, but have not soaked in water. I guess it keeps the husks from browning so much. Either way, it has a wonderful grilled flavor.

This has a nice flavor but it was too thin for my picky husband.  He doesn't like thin soups.  I am not sure what I could do different next time unless I cut the broth down to 3 cups.  I like it ok, but probably won't make again.
Most AMAZING bagels EVER!
Made these last night for a late night snack.  They were great.  The only thing I did different is add 1/2 tsp of cinnamon to the butter sugar mixture.  Very tasty.

I had been looking for this recipe ever since I saw it on Nigella's show. The bars are really easy to make. I didn't have the marshmallows at hand so I left them out of the recipe, and I replaced half of the digestive biscuits with equal volume of oats. They turned out quite good! Thanks Dr Gaellon!

sorry, this was way too sweet and chocolatey, if that is possible, even my kids didn't like it and they are chocolate fiends.  Won't make again.
Vanessa
Best whole grain bread I've made with or without a bread machine!  Love the seeds, love the texture and flavor, mixed in my bread machine and baked as a loaf in the oven.  Fabulous!

Excellent, easy, healthy salad. Was a great hit at our recent Xmas festivities.
I added in diced capsicum and baby spinach leaves for some extra colour and healthy goodness.
Thanks for the recipe!

Finally, a sweet potato recipe that doesn't require cooking the liquid before hand. Last time I tried that it came out like hard candy. This way is fool-proof and so much easier. Thanks for the recipe!

Had 10 for dinner on Sunday, had your Penne recipe as part of the Menu,it was teriffic, had only good comments. Will use regularly.
Thnks Mrs. EL.
Jess96

This dish was great!!! We have never had this type of veggie befor so I followed the recipe to the T. The italian breed crums gives the dish the extra kick of flavor.  I will make this dish agen. The recipe did not state when to put the crab in so I just put it in when I put the shrimp in.
Delicious salad, loved the dressing. Will certainly make again!

This is a wonderful salad recipe-my favorite, I make it at home all the time, very light and fresh tasting. I took it to a pot luck and it was enjoyed by everyone-kept getting requests for the recipe. One thing I would suggest is to add the dressing to the individual servings unless you know it will all be ate up, otherwise  it gets soggy and not good for leftovers-love this salad!!

These are very good.  They are incredibly moist, even 2 days after I made them.  Mine turned out almost cake like and my boyfriend said they taste like banana bread.  Next time, I am going to use splenda and lighten them up even more.  Thanks for sharing.
I accidentally rated this recipe.
I made this last night to serve with roast chicken breasts - VERY YUMMY!!! Easy to follow instructions.  I will make this again, it's a great dish - Thank you Elly for posting.
Great Enchilada Sauce! Excellent with Beef,Cheese,Chicken, and Bean Home made Enchiladas! Couldn't be easier or tastier! Thanks for sharing! Katie
This was really fun and cute.  Easy and good too!  I used the honey flaky biscuits and gave it a yummy sweetness to it!  This would be fun for birthdays too!

I've made this before without the cream cheese and only fruit cocktail.    But with the cheese, my friends asked me what i added aside from the cream.   I used more apples coz it added a little twist rather than using fruit cocktail all the time to this type of recipe and skipped the coconut.

I'm not a fan of fruit salad at all until I went to the Philippines and ate the fruit salad there.  It is to die for.  But no one would give me a recipe.  This recipe comes really close.  I couldn't find buko or any other canned or fresh young coconut, so I used the typical packaged shredded coconut.  This recipe has all the right flavors but the texture is a little off.  I doubled the amount of the coconut.  Still everyone oooohhhed and aaaahhhhed over the flavor.  As far as Nestles Cream, I found it and Nestles Sweetened and Condensed Milk in the Hispanic section of the Winco grocery store.   I can't wait to make another flan using those ingredients.  Even though the recipe still needs a little bit of tweaking.  I'll give it 5 stars.  I'm thrilled that I have something that comes that close to what I experienced in the PH.  Thank you for posting your recipe.

This was very tasty. We found it light and refreshing without being too filling. Needed quite a bit of salt and pepper though, and we even added a pinch of citric acid crystals to brighten it up a bit without adding actual lemon flavor. We thought it was better at room temperature than chilled.

I used this recipe by cutting way back and just using turnips and onions. Perfect on the timing for doneness. I do NOT advise using jarred minced garlic like another reader suggested. I added it only during the last 20 minutes and it almost burned. My suggestion would be to avoid the prepared stuff altogether and use fresh cloves, which is what I'll try next time.

Made these to go with Kittencal's Perfect Prime Rib Roast Beef. They were outstanding. There were no leftovers. Thanks for the recipe Bev, now everyone wants to know how I made them.
Deb

This is an amazing recipe! This makes these roots and swedes play together nicely, along with adding versatility to them. Good hot or cold: I've eaten this dish cold on salad greens with blue cheese drizzled on it, and it performed splendidly in a potpie with turkey after Thanksgiving. My vegetarian friends love this when I make it!

It was my first experience with uncooked Bok Choy.  I used powdered ginger, I was just out of fresh one. Otherwise I followed the recipe. The salad tasted wonderful (even though I usually don’t like ginger), it was very easy to prepare and it looked great. I’m planning to make it for my DH birthday party. Thank you for the recipe.

Very rich and yummy! I was looking far a recipe to use up some sun dried tomatoes packed in olive oil. I just drained them before I chopped them. I also just lightly browned the chicken and then added the onions and mushrooms so they all finished cooking together. Too rich to eat too often but was enjoyed by kids and adults both!
***I have since made this with diced fresh tomatoes in place of sun dried and also added the whole jar of pesto (almost doubled the recipe amount). Also very yummy!

I didn't have any sour cream on hand besides I try to cut the calories down as much as possible so I substituted FF plain Yogurt for the sour cream.  I used cream of mushroom soup as that is what I had on hand.  Used Pepper Jack cheese.  I thinned the sour cream, chicken, cheese mixture with a can, ( 14 oz), of Old El Paso Enchilada Sauce. and threw in a small can of sliced black olives into the mix.  Other than that I followed the recipe without the chilies. (ONLY because I didn't have any!)  This recipe I thought was good, but my DH thought it was fantastic!! That's why the 5 star rating. Will definitely make this again but put some habaneros or other hot chilies in it.  Thanks for the gr8 recipe.

I adore Italian regional cooking and was blown away by this sauce (including the eggplant)! Although I went the cheater route, this sauce begs for fresh chili peppers. Thank you for posting this recipe!

I also do potatoes like this, except I place a generous amount of salt on my baking sheet and set my potato on top of the salt.  It helps make the potato cook evenly all the way around, so no hard bottom.  I also pierce with fork before baking.
This is a really nice dish to make, it was easy and I had everything to make it. I would only change the yogurt at the end for sour cream. But it was really good as it was. Made for the ZWT4. Thanks

Yummy!  I used this as a base recipe, and made some of the changes that others did.  Also replaced some of the choc chips with raisins, and added some rolled oats and shredded carrots to get more fibre in them for my kids.  They were a hit!
This has to be the best way I've ever eaten asparagus!  Very flavorful.  Thanks!
This was very very good, I doubled up on the chili sauce and red pepper flakes, and think I will add more next time.  Thanks for the recipe and I will definately make it again!  :)

I made this with olive oil as I don't use margarine. The result was excellent.
This is an improvement on my old recipe which did not include mushrooms.
This was perfect.   I used Pam instead of margarine, and low fat mayonnaise.
Really liked the taste and the texture, but did think it was a bit dry.  Perhaps browning the onions and mushrooms first or adding a bit more oil would help, but overall very good!

What a terrific way the serve cabbage!! I did find the cheese was a little bit thick, next (and there will be a next time!!) I will add a little more milk. On the whole a marvellous recipe!! Thanks for posting!!
Wonderful - even my hubby enjoys this (and he is a picky eater)

I've made this a few times now and my kids, aged between 8 and 1, still love it! I leave out the chili powder, and have used a little mild taco sauce when I don't have enough salsa. Other than that I follow the recipe and it's fantastic. I like to serve it on top of a pile of mashed potatoes. Yum!
Would definately make this again.  Cooked it on the stovetop only because I forgot to throw it into the crockpot before going to work.  Would be an easy thing to bring to a potluck.
I thought this tasted fantastic. The kids didn't seem to be as enthusiastic about it as me, but they did like it and wanted it again.

I was lucky enough this past Saturday to come across freshly baked wheat/herb pita bread at my grocery store,which was wonderful with this little "pizza!"  I used lots of bacon and added some sauteed mushrooms that I had left over from dinner the night before.  I used my mandoline to get the onions as thin as possible and the sauce was killer!  Loved it Heather!  Thank you!

These are my new favorite brownie. They are perfection!! An offset spatula really helps with spreading the filling (since it is fairly thick and hard to spread) and topping (since it has to be spread very thin).
This was very good.

I used 6 chicken tenderloins, 1/4 c flour,1/4 c sesame seed,1/4T paprika, 1/4 t of salt and 1/4 t poultry seasoning, also used a whole egg and a splash of milk.  It was excellent, albeit a little dry,made sweet & sour sauce, fried rice, using the leftover egg /milk from the chicken coating to scramble in, and steamed carrots.  Beautiful presentation, will make again & again, Thanks CJay
Very nice moist loaf cake. Not too sweet and a good chocolate flavour. Be careful not to overbake!

This is absolutely delicious!  It's also very versatile -- spectacular with butter and marmalade, but also a perfect accompaniment to soup and hearty bean spread.  I was a bit surprised when it first came out of the machine - it's dense (and therefore small), so I thought I'd measured something wrong.  The texture is firm and chewy, very different from American-style cornbreads which are quick breads, not yeast-based.  But the flavor knocked my socks off, and it keeps better than American cornbreads do, too.  I had 2 friends over for dinner and the 3 of us gobbled it all up in about 2 seconds flat, and I found myself mixing up another 2-pound (rather than the original 1-pound) loaf for my bread machine within an hour of the first one being done.
Fabulous and easy.  I did think the orange zest was a bit overpowering, though.  I'd cut back on it in the future.  But this is definitely a keeper, and it makes a lot.
This was wonderfully, surprisingly decadent and delicious!! I am not a big fan of rhubarb, but was persuaded by DH to make this and I am certainly glad I did! Thanks for posting this gem!

Thanks, for the recipe.
I am a diabetic and using the Splenda really helped.
I put all of the filling into a deep dish pie crust and it turned out great.
My husband even liked it.
Thanks, again, Redneck.
P. S. I baked it for the full 45 minutes.

This was good, but I agree it needed a bit more "oomph." I used white wine and added a bit of heavy cream after it was done to make more of a cream sauce. Using meat ravioi instead of cheese also adds some more flavor. Good basic recipe that allows for lots of alterations.

I have substituted walnuts for the pecans as well as using orange juice in place of the liquor. Also, we don't use fresh herbs much so I checked the internet to find out how much dry sage to use compared to fresh. Word of warning - as the recipe indicates, this makes a LOT!

Note:  This recipe comes from allrecipes. I have had it saved in my recipe box there since 2003.
Great flavor!  Mine came out a little bit crumbly but the flavor was great.
I have been using this recipe since it was published in Bon Appetit. It is quick and easy to make, and absolutely delectable. Even better the day after!
I was so excited to share how much I loved this I forgot to rate it!  Thanks again!!  :)

Very easy and YUMMY!  My BF raved for 4 days about the delicious chicken breasts I cooked using this marinade.  I used basalmic vinegar and garlic powder instead of garlic salt, omitted the regular salt, and marinated for 20  minutes before broiling my boneless chicken breasts for 5 minutes each side.  FANTASTIC!!!

I used this marinade on chicken wings. Subsituted with mayo but kept everything else as per recipe. I was very surprised with the taste since I only had my chicken wings in this for maybe 10 mins! Wonderful, will keep making this one! Thanks!

My whole family loved it. I thought it was going to be too lemony but it was perfect! I made extra and let it marinade for the next day. Very easy and tasty. I did cut out half of the miracle whip since I didn't have the light version. And only used 1/2 C. of oil. It still was yummy! 
Thanks for sharing.
Thanks for the great recipe - it was the best chicken I ever made. Flavorful, moist, just delicious!
I really thought the marinade would be greasy b/c of the mayo, but it wasn't at all. Really tasty.  Used dried thyme (2t) but otherwise changed nothing. E/one loved it!
This is wonderful!  I did it just as the recipe directed.  The chicken was tender and very flavorful!  Thanks for a fabulous twist on traditional chicken!
It was OK and something a little different from my usual.

This is really, really good!  I've used it the last two times I've made chicken breasts and will use it again tonight.  So much better (and cheaper) than bottled marinade.  I cut the recipe in half for two chicken breasts.  Left the salt out completely and I've substituted Rosemary for the Thyme once (I ran out) and still very tasty.
Best marinade ever! My whole family loves it.
Very tasty and moist!  And all ingredients I keep on hand - a major plus!
Super! I've tried with the Dijon and without and it's great both ways. Just a great marinade!
This is easy and taste wonderful.  I make it to impress my company. Thanks

As already posted. They are tomatoes with herbs and as a plate filler they look good. Not the "wow" part of a dish but if you need something to fill a plate for your family during the week its a good option.

Came back from NOLA from a vacation centered around all of the NO foods I love. I ate red beans and rice from the Praline Connection (off Frenchman) 3 times because it was sooo good. When I came home I went thru withdrawls. These taste just like the ones I had over there. I made them twice in one week to kill my craving. One recommendation is to use converted rice (Uncle Ben's) for your rice portion in this recipe to keep it authentic. Oh and I don't add the salt, hock, or sausage until the beans have cook for at least one hour. Salt can sometimes mess with the tenderness of the beans if added too soon. thanks for the recipe!

This has become a standard recipe that I make every weekend for the coming week. I crush the branflakes with a potato masher before adding the milk. Also The raisins are a great addition. I use paper muffin cups and lightly spray them with Pam so that the paper comes off without sticking to the muffin.

This is intense and subtle at the same time.  The flavour is unexpectly a big WOW! The cumin is subtle and the citrus is more defined but work so well together.  I do not like cooked spinach but really enjoyed this.  I will be trying it at home pronto!  Many thanks Irmgard.  Another winner!

This was wonderful.  I will admit to browing and adding a couple of lamb lollypops an hour or two before serving.  I also threw in the entire sausage.   I did go 1/2 a cup less on the liquid and it was still very soupy.  Maybe this recipe is better on the stove that way, but a little corn starch boiled down on the stove made it divine.  Thank you for such a great tasting recipe!

This is a really great dip. My husband and I had the munchies and didn't want to cook a heavy meal so this was our solution. Very rich and the seasoning was perfect. We also liked the crunchy texture. We spread it on wheat crackers and were happy as could be. Thank-you Manda.
i loved this! it's like old-school vegetarian (brown rice and tofu) with a nice zip coming from the cilantro. nice textures and a refreshing blend of flavors. thanks!
Delicious, easy and great with grilled cheese. My son said, "This is really good soup!" I think I'd use a little less milk next time but other than that - awesome!

This soup was so good! As suggested, I did use a bit more flour to make a thicker soup(probably about 1/3 cup). I also added some basil to the soup, which gave it a nice spin. Instead of serving with cheese, I served with Cheeze-Its, as I like a little crunch in my soup, and the crackers made it even more delicious! I will be making this again.
so good! didn't have tomato juice so I used about 20oz of tomato sauce and 3/4 cup of water. love how you can controll the salt! so easy I will not be buying a can of cambells! thankst for posting.
thanks for the recipe. .easy and delish!

YUM!!!! tried another version of cream of tomato soup and it curdled, made it again following strict instructions and it still curdled. This one was so easy and SUPER delicious! I used a cup of milk and a cup of half and half instead of 3 cups of milk. NEVER going back to campbells!
Excellent recipe.  I substituted plain almond milk for my family with dairy allergies.  Tasted delicious!
a good one

This is a very nice Bok Choy recipe.  I took Derf's advice and used only juice of 1/2 lemon and added shredded carrots (store bought).  Next time I will slice my own carrots as the store bought does not have enough taste.  My DH and I really enjoyed the combination of flavors.  Thanks, Brewster Baxter, for sharing the recipe with us.
These were very good.  I was glad to see a recipe that kept the vegetables healthy instead of covering up the taste with bacon or cream.  Great recipe.
These definitely brought me closer to liking this vegetable! I added some cubed pancetta to the mix and it helped, as well.
I found some wonderful brussels sprouts on the stalk and just had to find the perfect way to prepare them.  Thanks so much for this wonderful recipe!
This was great.  I ended mixing the sauce in but we all liked it, even the kids.
I make this recipe all the time. It's a favorite in my house. Recently, I used the ingredients and made turkey meatballs and glazed them with the sauce. Absolutely delicious. Thank you!
A friend had oatmeal pie back east and told me about it.  I tried this recipe and everyone loved it!
I enjoyed the taste, but had trouble with it setting up. I used a mixture of quick and Irish oats that I had on hand so that could be it. I will make again as is.

I made this of an office party and it was a hit!  I also had problems getting it it set up right and will try cooking a little longer next time - maybe 1 hr instead of 45 min. Even my picky, picky daughter loved it.  It's a keeper!

I wish I could fit gallon size jug of pickles in my fridge! I cut the recipe in half, used white onions & added xtra dried whole peppers. I made 'em  3 weeks ago  &  I let them soak for 1 week. They are all gone, ready to make more! OH, the onions! So good and spicy! I ate them with everything! the girls at work wanna try them deviled! can't wait! Thanks, Pot Scrubber, for taking me back to my childhood! I  remember it was a great day when the store clerk let me dig out my own pickled egg from the jug!
Maybe I did something wrong, but this just wasn't that good.  My teenage boys didn't like it either.  I followed the directions exactly, used Pace picante sauce.
This was an easy way to have Mexican at the table. They turned out great! I served them with mexicorn.
Holy Smokes!  There's a reason why this was under Top 40 Recipes.  Sooooo tasty, so easy, so quick!  I have given this recipe out three times in the last week.  Thanks so much!

Wow!  These are fantastic.  My husband used to live in Arizona and loves Chimichangas, but always complained that he couldn't find good chimis where we now live.  He absolutely raved about these, we are definitely going to make these regularly.  My 8 yr old son is a picky eater, and loves these also.  

The only change I made was to mince the chicken up fine.  

Thank you for the recipe.
These are great chimichangas

WOW! What a great recipe!! Tastes like I ordered it off of a menu at a famous Mexican Restaurant! I followed the recipe exactly.  I did make (3) with wheat tortillas and they were great also! Very Easy to make too! THANKS!!!

These are the BEST!!!  I am a chimi"aholic" and these are great.  I used shredded beef as I had no chicken.  YUMMY!!  I sprayed the tops with a canola oil spray and they browned nicely and crispy.  I topped it with sour cream, salsa and cheese.  Can't wait to try with the chicken which is my favorite.  Thank you-thank you-thank you...

These were really good. I didn't add onion because I didn't have any and I used oil spray instead of using butter and they turned out great. I think next time I might add some mushrooms and some red or green peppers along with some garlic to add some more flavour. Thanks for posting.

I'm giving this 5 stars because my 2 1/2 year old is gobbling them up, no questions asked.

I didn't have green onions on hand so I chopped up 1/2 a yellow onion and threw it in the food processor along with some cooked chicken to make shredded chicken.

These are very tasty.  I will definatly make these again and again.

I think they'd freeze well, too, and be great for reheating in the oven.

When I cooked Oven-fried Chicken Chimichangas for first time my family fell in love with it immediatly and asked me for more and more! Then I cooked it for my picnic party and all my friends just wowed and asked for the recipe! I cook Chimichangas for my family quite often now and brave enough to make different variations of this wonderful basic recipe! As for my friends, I gave them this link :)
Thank you Miss Annie for sharing!
Great tasting and easy to make. I used whole wheat tortillas for a healthy crunch. My husband and I ate all 6 chimichangs by ourselves!
Family loved these and so easy to make with leftover chicken! Only thing I did differently was shred the chicken with 2 forks :)

These chimichangas are awesome!!!  I had some cooked chicken in the freezer and decided to make these after I read the reviews, and I happened to have all the other ingredients too.  I took one to work and did not have time to warm it up but it didn't matter, they're great cold too!  I will definitely make these again and freeze them.  They're easy to make and, if you're having company over, you can prepare them to the point of just before baking them and  then just slip them in the oven when you're ready.  Great tasting & healthy too!

Really enjoyed these.  I made them for dinner tonight and they were well received.  I used a mild salsa made by TGIFridays because I myself just cannot handle spicy food.  I stuck to the original recipe the rest of the way.  One person at the dinner table did say they wished they had been a little spicier, but then, that was on me.  I served these with yellow rice that was a little more spiced up than usual and topped with additional salsa and cheddar cheese.  Overall, a good weeknight meal.  Thanks for the recipe Ms. Annie!

I've made this twice already for my family, and they can't get ENOUGH!! My father, who eats out quite often, said this is even better than the best Mexican restaurant he's been to, and if they made it like this he'd actually enjoy the food more often! This recipe has earned me the coveted "Wow, she can cook" remark :D Definetly a new staple of my cookbook!

These were scrumptious and incredibly easy and quick to put together.  The people I made them for raved and said that it was just as good, if not better, than anything they've had at their favorite Mexican restaurants.  The flavor and spicyness was just right and considering what you're eating they're reasonably healthy too.  Gotta love that.
These were great.  They were fast and yummy.

I cannot say enough about these "chims". My husband has been asking when are we having them next since the day I made them. I did not have salsa so used hot sauce instead and they were wonderful so I can just imagine what they will taste like with the salsa. Very delectable and is definelty a new addition to our rotation.

mades these for dinner the other night - excellent!  followed recipe to a T.  used real butter and came out nice and crispy and browned.  reheated the next night beautifully.  wonder if you could freeze them after they've baked?  definitely will make again.  very easy to prepare.  thumbs up from the kids who are picky (although next time i may use finely chopped onions for their sake as they picked out the green onions).

I thought that these were outstanding. The flavour was fantastic and had the perfect texture. I expected the tortilla to be crispy but it was nice and soft which made them even better. Thanks for sharing the recipe. you have made one Edinburgh family very happy.

Very good, and easy to make. I think next time I'll try using pepper jack cheese instead of cheddar, since it seemed a bit out of place, and topping with some fresh crumbly queso (you can get it at the Hispanic grocery store).
Wow!  This was so good.  The only thing that I did differently was to add some monty-jack cheese also.  I very good alternative to the traditional heavy/fried chimichangas.
Love it!
These are just awesome.  They come out of the oven perfectly crisp, but not oily, like you would get from frying.  I like to put salsa and sour cream when I plate them.

These chimichangas are so delicious and very easy to put together. I used shredded chicken, just a personal preference and served with sour cream and guacamole on top and spanish rice on the side. What a tasy dish!
These were super good! We didn't even put anything on top of them and they were still yummy! The spices were just right for our family. Thanks!
These were easy and good. I liked baking them instead of frying.

Very very easy to make and OH so delicious!

I made a few changes: used medium salsa, which made it really spicy! But, it was good. Omitted the onions and picante. Instead of margarine I used EVOO spray and they turned out nice and crispy!
Served it with some spanish rice and black beans!

This will definitely be added to the regular meals list! Thanks!
We really liked these.  I shredded the chicken and used fresh pico de gallo.  I also used a little extra pico de gallo.  Otherwise, I made them exactly as described and they were so good!  Thank you!
I made this using hamburger since I did not have any chicken . I used the picante sauce and half a package of taco seasoning my family loved them! I can not wait to try it with the chicken

These were great!  I used ground beef, but will try chicken next time with some fresh cilantro.  My only word of warning:  Be sure to leave time for these babies to cool before eating!  I mis-timed my side dishes, so ended up taking them about of the oven about 10 minutes before everything else was ready, but even after sitting out for 10-15 minutes, the filling was way too hot to eat.

I've made these countless times over the last year, and they are a hit everytime with my husband.  They are so easy to prepare with simple ingredients.  I sometimes add rice if I have it leftover.  There's only 2 of us so there are always a few my husband can take to work with him for lunch the next day.  Thanks for posting!
Good chimichangas, nice flavor and easy to make.  We'll be making these again, next time adding some green peppers to the filling.
YUM! I garnished mine with shredded lettuce, few diced tomatos and sour cream. I will be making again!
very tasty, good flavor and kids loved it.  Will certainly make again.
My husband loved these, but we both agreed next time we'd try to kick them up a notch with the help of more spices and/or hot sauce.  Thank you!

This made a great lunch!  I used that salsa that has corn and beans in it.  The salsa was really mild, so I got some spicy re-fried beans (the mushy stuff in the can) and spread it on the tortillas before adding the mixture.  I would recomend using a spicy salsa or picante sauce.  It was yum!

Great recipe, Miss Annie! Easy to make and very delicious. I poached the chicken and then shredded. Also added fresh cilantro to the chicken mixture. Everyone (husband and all four kids) loved it as well!
Thank you!

I used Morningstar Farms vegetarian "chicken" meal starters for this recipe instead of the chicken. I keep kosher and we don't mix meat and dairy, so this little substitution on your recipe made a quick but very yummy dinner for my husband and I. Thanks so much!

Sorry, but my family didn't enjoy this as much as I expected.  It wasn't the flavor of what was there, it just that there was something missing.  I served it with salsa, sr crm, and lettuce and still seemed like it needs a sauce of some sort.  Maybe an enchilada gravy. It did crisp up nicely. I followed the recipe exactly, no substitutions except salsa instead of picante.  I may try again using shredded beef instead of chicken.
These were absolutely delicious and quite simple to make!  I wouldn't change anything in the recipe- it was just right as it is written.  I will definitely be making this again!
This recipe looks awesome I cant wait to get home and try it. I'll write back later thanks!

This was great!  I too did my own thing with it.  I added mexicorn, black beans, cilantro and a chipolte pepper.  Brushed with olive oil it was so crunchy.....served with a fresh salsa I made and sour cream.  Can't wait to make again and again YUMMY!!!

If you love chimies, you must try this. I like to top mine off with lettuce, tomatoes, and sour cream. There is just something about the hot and cold combination that I really like. As others have said, you will be so surprised how crunchy the tortillas turn out. I sprayed mine with butter flavored Pam and they turned out great. Thank you so much for this outstanding recipe.

I love finding recipes that I can follow for my DH but can also adapt to vegetarian for me.  I made the filling except I didn't include the chicken.  I then divided the mixture among 5 tortilla shells.  I cubed one cooked chicken breast and put it on top of the filling on 3 shells and on the remaining 2 shells, I used Lightlife ready to use chick'n style strips (seasoned veggie strips).  After baking, we topped the chimis with tomatoes, lettuce and sour cream.  Yummalicious!  Thanks for a great recipe.
Our family very much enjoyed this recipe!!  I cooked in our convection oven and it made the tortillas very crispy, as if they were fried.  Thanks for the post, this will be a staple in our home!

soooo good! I made these for a Bible study group and everybody really enjoyed them. I sauteed some bell pepper and regular onion (instead of green onions) together before mixing everything together. I guess I used bigger tortillas, because I used closer to 1/2 C per tortilla and fewer tortillas. Oh yeah, I also added some cilatro to the mix too. Thanks for a great recipe! ~

Terrific recipe! Thanks for sharing. Like others, I was looking for a healthier version of chimichangas, and these fit the bill. I made a couple of adjustments. I used shredded turkey breast for the filling, didn't season the meat very much (just added a bit of green chile sauce), and stuffed the tortillas with as much filling as they would reasonably hold. I brushed with canola oil and cooked in a countertop broiler oven. Note to others: if you choose this method, reduce the heat significantly (maybe around 350F) and be prepared to cover the chimis with a piece of foil to avoid burning. I topped the chimis with warm green chile sauce, cheese and sour cream and served with pinto beans seasoned with cumin and oregano (to incorporate the flavors of the original recipe). It was a great meal, and my husband said he thought these chimis were as good as his favorite restaurant ones! Thanks again!

I can't stop making these!  The filling is absolutely amazing and its hard for me to stop eating it long enough to put it in the tortillas!  I did add some more picante sauce and a tad bit more cheese, but that was the only difference.  I've made it several times now and it is fast becoming a part of our weekly menu.  Thanks for a great recipe!
These were delicious and I loved the fact that you didn't have to deep fry them. Thank you!

This was a big hit with my family and a recipe my husband said to "please" make again! The tortillas came out of the oven crispy and the filling was delicious. I love recipes that are versatile, when you can use a variety of ingredient combinations -- and this one is a great example. Next time I think I'll use beef, or maybe I'll add some black beans. This would be great to make ahead then bake 30 minutes before dinnertime. Love it!

these were so yummy. The first time I made them just as the recipe said, and then the second time I softened each tortilla in oil before putting in the chicken mixture and baking. doing so made the chimichangas actually taste more like they would at a restaurant...although adding extra calories (which I thought was well worth it!) thanks for a great recipe.
I tried this recipe and it was very good, I added some frozen corn and brushed tops with butter instead of margarine.  Next time I think I will add some black beans, great recipe and great taste.
Love these.

Oh my gosh, this was so good.  I knew my husband and son would love it but I am very picky and not sure if "I" would like it but I thought it was great.  My son and husband did love it and ate every single one, I am lucky I got to try the one I did.  Will make again, my son has already asked.  Thank you
Tried these and the whole family enjoyed them. Lots of flavor. Will definately do again.

These were very good.  Just what you would expect from the ingredients; I think you could play with the filling to make it just how you like.  The best part though is how by covering the rolled up tortilla with butter and baking it, it tastes and has a very similar texture to a true deep-fried chimichanga.
Perfecto

I have a recipe at home much like this but I serve Chipotle Sour Cream and Jalapeno jelly on the side.  If I would have had the prepared sauces to go with these, I would have enjoyed them even more.  I like that you don't deep fry them, and they are crispy
These were great!  Definitely something I would make again!

WOW! I used left over London Broil taco mest (in a crock pot with taco seasoning YUM!) This was delicious and OH so easy! I use the Rachel Ray 'Sister's favorite fajita's' black bean recipe from Food network. Kids had bean and cheese buritos and dad and I loved these! Can't wait to have these again! Thank you!

I can't give this any stars (like it needs more anyway!) because I swapped the chicken in the recipe for recipe #169500.  I did want to comment on this method for making chimichangas...it was wonderful!  I didn't miss frying them at all!  The chicken from the other recipe was also fabulous in these!  To save time next time, I will try the recipe as written and then come back to post the five stars that I'm sure it deserves!

I have made this recipe many times and just realized I never reviewed it. I love this recipe!! It is so easy to make and I usually have everything right in my cupboard. I have made this recipe exactly as written and it is is great. This time though I tried as some others suggested and sprayed with olive oil instead of the margarine and from now on this is the way I'm going to do it. I think it gave it a little extra crunch. Either way thank you for a wonderful recipe Miss Annie!!
We loved the crispy crust on these!  They were absolutely delicious with sour cream on the side.  Thank you!

Fantastic!  I used enchilada sauce instead of the picante, trying to match my favorite comfort food from a local Mexican restaurant, and this was spot-on!  We will definitely be making this again and again!  Great use for left-over chicken!

Thank you so much for this recipe. I have made it several times and my family absolutely loves it. The first time I made exactly as written and the chimis turned out great. The next few times I have used what I had on hand and they are always good. Just a couple things I found. Tortillas have a smooth side and a blistered  (bumpy) side. The chimis get crisper if the smooth side is out. Also I use parchment paper so I don't have a sticking issue. Really good. Thank you!
Maybe it was the type or tortilla I used (Trader Joe's), but these came out crunchy after only 13 minutes.  It tasted great but wasn't a chimichanga.
This was soo yummy! I wound up making it again the very next night. I used Pace Picante sauce (hot). Thank you!!
Used some leftover rotisserie chicken with this recipe.  It turned out to be a very moist meal and a great way to make healthier chimichangas.
YUM!  My husband and I loved these.  I used a mild salsa the first time but will use a medium salsa next time to add a bit of a kick.  Thanks for a great recipe!

We made these for dinner tonight instead of going to the Mexican restaurant right down the street...and they were just as delicious!!!  I used chopped chicken breasts.  I also seasoned my chicken with garlic powder, onio powder, chili powder, and cayenne pepper before cooking it as mrs. mcb suggested!  I didn't have any green onions, so I just used plain yellow onions that I chopped up.  I used Pace Picante sauce.  Mine cooked up in 20 minutes.  At about 18 minutes, I sprinkled a little more cheese on top and melted...soo good!  I served with sour cream.  Thanks for a great recipe!

These things are f***ing tasty! I made mine with beef I cooked in the crockpot with all the spices (cumin, red pepper, red pepper flakes, cinnamon, cocoa, and tabasco) I had on hand plus a little salsa.
These are awesome and so easy to make!  I used chicken, but I don't see why you couldn't use beef or pork.  This recipe is going on my cooking blog as a definite keeper.

This are really good!!! Despite all of the good reviews I was still nervous about how these wold turn out. I did everything exactly the same except I used I cant believe its not butter spray butter.  Thanks for a great recipe
Followed your recipe as is. These are really good! Boyfriend and I had enough for lunch and dinner the next day =)
My whole family loved these and so did I--made it exactly as written. Thank you for a quick meal that is also delicious.
Very good! I served with brown rice and I brushed them with olive oil while cooking to get them to crisp. Yum!
Hubby and I really enjoyed these, and will make them again and again.

WOW!!! This is soooo easy to make and it taste GREAT. Heats up great too. I used HOT salsa and I added corn and mushrooms. It makes 6-8 Chimichangas so have a few extra tortilla ready. I used spray Pam instead of margarine. AMAZING!!! Thanks for sharing. :)

Yum!  My family loved this recipe!  I made a few changes: I didn't have green onions, so I omitted those, but added a can of green chiles.  I also added a can of red kidney beans (drained and rinsed) as well as a bit more salsa.  I substituted butter for margarine, shredded colby/jack cheese for cheddar and served with sour cream.  Will definitely make again!

Great starter recipe! I bought a rotisserie chicken at the store and shredded that first. I added some pepper and garlic salt along with the cumin and oregano. I also added diced green peppers along with the green onions. Used mexican cheese and green enchilada sauce. I let it sit in the fridge for an hour before I rolled it in tortilla. I used evoo and heated it as directed. I wish I had sour cream to go with it but it turned out great! Also used this as an enchilada dish by just adding green enchilada sauce and cheese on top before putting it in the oven. I served it with corn and sour cream. Will make this again.

This recipe is in My Top Five!  I  used Olive Oil for Margarine, 4 chesse mexican blend cheese for Cheddar and a fancy Tortillas that has a touch of Corn in it.  Served it with Spanish rice, Veg Beans & lots of Sour Cream.  The hubby who is a meat and potatoes guy went wild.  He usually wont eat things topped with cheese.  I loved it as well and I am the type of cook who usually does not eat dinner.  Once I cooked it I no longer really want to eat!!  This could easily be something I cook weekly. Thank you Annie
these were awesome! i used ground beef instead of chicken and i added red peppers.. the sauce was great! thanks for the recipe=)
What a quick and yummy dinner.  All it took was 2 chicken breasts to equal 4 good size burritos.  Will definitely make it again!  Used picante salsa and it was great!
What a quick and easy recipe. So much healthier than deep frying without losing the flavor.
Awesome! Thats pretty much all I have to say!
very tasty and easy to make. I followed directions as written except used olive oil as a previous reviewer suggested. turned out crunchy and delish! will make again.

Excellent basic recipe.... One hint - I warmed the tortilla shells in the microwave for about 10 seconds.  It enabled me to "fold" the sides in without breakage.  Next time I try it I am going to add olives or guacamole.  Wonderful!  Thanks for posting.

Being exiled to the middle of nowhere with no ethnic food to speak of (there isn't even a taco bell for 4 hours for a pale imitation of mexican) isn't fun.  THIS recipe makes it ALOT easier.  My family and I loved them - I want to try them with ground beef next time.  Thank you so much for the recipe!

Amazing flaky and crisy tortilla shell!  This was such a fun and yummy meal...I want to try it with shredded roast beef next time!!  I omitted the oregano as we don't care for it (added some pepper and a bit of garlic salt as recommended), halved the cumin, and used regular onion as i didn't have any green onion on hand.  It was very good and not bland at all. Thanks for sharing!!

These were wonderful. They cooked up golden brown and crispy. Not a soggy bite to be found. 

I used lean ground beef instead of chicken and added taco seasoning. 

Make extra and freeze, they reheat very well.

Good Golly Miss Annie, what a great recipe!!  I don't know why I haven't made them sooner!!  I did add more garlic, cumin and a pinch of taco seasoning.  Brushed with melted butter and baked on oven stone.  Sure enough,  crispy on the outside and not 'doughy' tasting at all.  Made a small bit of a butter/flour roux, adding some pepper jack cheese. Served with a dollop of my "cheese sauce" on top plus salsa.  Beans and rice rounded out the plate.  Wonderful!!  Thank you for posting.

The whole family loved this! I had cooked alot of chicken so I ended up tripling the batch. I only added 1/2 t. cumin because my dad and I don't like it. I also filled the tortillas with 1/2 c. of filling, 1/4 was not enough. I only cooked for 20 minutes and they were slightly crispy, perfect doneness. Even my 1 year old liked it and I can't get her to eat anything with texture. Thanx for an amazing recipe! Next time I'm going to try with flavored tortillas.
Sorry, my family and I just didn't like it.  I made it exactly as directed.  Seemed to have too much picante sauce and not enough cheese or other flavorful ingredients.
I have made these several times.  Due to DH's preference, I omit the salsa (he'd rather dip it in).  Yum!
This has made it into a regular rotation. We use the seasoned chicken from this recipe for all kinds of Mexican meals.
Wonderful!  They truly tasted and had the texture of being deep fried, even though they weren't.

I gave this recipe the full 5 stars for it's ease and passing the taste test of my entire family!  In addition to making the chicken ones, I used up some left over ground beef for some beef chimis too. My young boys loved those.  I did end up covering them w/aluminum foil the last half of cooking time as they were getting too brown on the edges.  A keeper!
Very good. :)
Delicious! Hubby loved them. This will make a nice change into the typical meal rotation at our house!
Easy and absolutely wonderful recipe!  Thank you for sharing.

We loved them!  I made 8 and followed other reviewers' advice to brush them with olive oil instead of butter.  I was so excited to have leftovers to take to work for lunch.  Even my picky 4-year-old said "I love 'em!" . . . once I convinced her to try them.  Thanks for a yummy easy dinner recipe!
Yummy sounds heard round the table!  Easy and delish. Thank you Miss Annie!

I love these!  I double the recipe and add a can of black beans.  Then I like mine full so I use about a half cup of filling in each.  We have a small family and while I do enjoy cooking there is plenty of nights that I just want something easy so I make these one night bake only what we eat and put the rest on a pan and freeze individually.  Once frozen I put in a big baggy and when I'm lazy I grab an uncooked chimichanga out of the freezer brush with melted butter and cook just like I would a freshly made one.  With the doubled recipe and 1/2 cup filling I still end up with 12 chimichangas. Thanks to the poster for making my life a little easier while still eating great food!

This is soooo good! gy onions so I used about 1 tsp of onion powder, and added a little shake of garlic powder. I added a can of black beans, about 1 cup of frozen corn, and a 4 oz can of green chilies. Since im at 9,000 ft above sea level, things dry out quicker, so I added about 1/3 cup of Taco Bell Mild Sauce. About 5 mins before it was done, i added some sliced black olives and shredded cheese also to the top. This is so amazing, and plan on making this when my mom comes and visits in a couple weeks. :) Oh yeah, and used olive oil instead of margarine.

I have to admit, I was really skeptical about this recipe and all the 5-star ratings it had received. But I decided to make this (my sister made it, actually) because it seemed easy and cheap. Well, let me tell you, one bite and I knew it was a keeper!

We had about 3-4 cups of cooked shredded chicken, so my sister doubled the rest of the ingredients. We didn't change anything else about the recipe, except for doubling it, of course.
This recipe was passed on to me by a friend today and it is GOOD!  Very easy to make and very tasty.
Cookies in a snap! These are really good, especially while they are still warm. Thanks for posting this!

A friend of mine had given me this recipe a year ago when I got married and I just tried it today. They are excellent cookies. I still think I prefer the peanut butter cookies that require the flour, brown sugar, etc. But, definitely a keeper.
Further to last comment - yummy with 1/2 cup choc chips thrown in!! (You can tell I'm addicted to this recipe!:)

These are very good.  I substituted 3/4 cup Splenda for the cup of sugar, and they did fine.  And I only use creamy PB, no problem.
Another time when I made them, I added 3 or 4 teaspoons of cocoa powder. They are good made this way too, but use the full measure of sugar or Splenda.

These were so easy to make, I only had a little peanut butter left so halfed the ingredients and adjusted the cooking time to nine minutes.  I also added vanilla essense as per other reviews.  The end result was attractive but slighly sickly- I couldn't eat very many of these cookies in one go as much as I wanted to!

Yes!  This is a wonderful recipe and I use cookie stamps when I make this, instead of the criss-cross with the fork.  With no flour, these cookies retain the cookie stamp design beautifully!  Think I'll go make some now!

SO EASY AND TASTY!  I put a few mini chocolate kisses on the top when they came out of the oven.  These were so good that we ate the whole batch in one night between the two of us!  I have tried making this before and they didn't turn out great but that is because I was not flattening the dough with a fork before baking it.  As another reviewer wrote, dipping the fork in water really helps.  I will be making more of these ASAP! Thanks!

These are great with the addition of 1/4 teaspoon of salt.  Try them spread with strawberry jam while they're still warm!
EDIT: The first time I made these, I used natural peanut butter (only peanuts + salt).  I made them again with Jiff, and I didn't like them at all - they were WAY too sweet, with a strange texture.  So I would definately recommend natural peanut butter in this recipe.
soooooo good, i used half brown and half gran. sugar i rolled into a ball then pressed down, i also added mini chocolate chips, sooo yummy!
I made these for my boyfriend last weekend and he has demanded I make them again! Thanks for a yummy (and easy!) recipe!

These are too easy and very good! I had heard of this before, but didn't have the nerve to try it until I needed to make cookies but didn't have all the ingredients on hand. I'm making these for Christmas with the kisses on top.

Very Yummy! Thanks. I love that it doesn't take many ingredients to make great peanut butter cookies and the kids love that they take no time at all. 
Thanks for sharing.
This is just too easy to taste this good. Why did I ever make them another way?
Amazing that this works- great- thanks!
they were edable but not soft and not hard they were to chewy.
So simple and taste great!
So simple and so delicious!  Thank you!
I made many different varieties of cookies for Christmas. I'm sorry to say that these were still left-over weeks after and I threw the remainder away. They were just too sweet and were disappointing.

On the day I made these cookies, I had already made two batches, but I was really in a baking mood, and these were so simple that I decided to make these at the last minute.  I am so glad I did!  These were the best of the three kinds I made, and definitely the quickest and easiest. Yum.
Great recipe ~ always have these ingredients on hand!  Key is not letting them brown.

I think the people who didn't like this recipe must have done something wrong.  I loved these...they were nice and chewy and tasty.  I used half brown sugar and half regular sugar and a tsp of vanilla.  I think also that if I would have rolled them in sugar before baking, it would have made them too sweet, but without the sugar, they were just right.  I will definitely make these again!!

Thx this is exactly what I was looking for because it was my first time making pb cookies ... these were easy and tastey and satisfied our craving tonight.  I added a few mini chocolate chips.  Thx 4 sharing :)
Easy to make and tasted okay.  I just needed alittle more cookie and a little less peanut butter.
These were super fast and really good. I didn't think that they would turn out very well, but was quite happily surprised. Thanks for a great recipe.
pretty good, kind of dense, but very easy

These were good but not the best peanut butter cookies I've ever had. Very easy to make though. Careful you don't keep them in too long. My second cookie sheet got done before they'd been in 10 minutes, but that might have just been my oven.
I thought these were just "ok".  They were definitely fast and easy but were heavy and didn't really store well- the next day they were hard.  I won't make them again.
Super Super Easy!  Great for a quick snack!

Talk about tasting peanut butter...Wow!
Easy to make I love it.

My favorite cookie recipe. I always make these when I need cookies in a pinch because I always have the ingredients on hand and they are oh so good! I agree that you have to be careful not to over cook these.
WOW best ones ever..

These are pretty good.  If I'm in a rush to make cookies, I'll probably reach for this recipe.  I used a low carb, smooth peanut butter, and it worked well.  I also added Hershey's Special Dark Chocolate chips to half of the dough, and I prefer those!  Thanks for posting!
Excellent! I top them with a chocolate kiss when they come out of the oven, if I have them on hand, if not, they're great all on their own.  Thanks!

I did not like these cookies. They were too sweet and crumbly. We ended up having to add butter, flour, and baking soda to the remaining dough to make the cookies better. I did use natural peanut butter, so I'm not sure if that had an effect. It should have made them less sweet, because natural peanut butter has no sugar or high fructose corn syrup, but they still tasted like pure sugar.
So easy & So yummy & eaten up so very quickly.
These were great! All gone at the block party within a 1/2 hour!

soooo easy! Within 20 min I had cookies and kids looking for what smelled so good!
I used chunky PB witch gave them an extra crunch! I will use less sugar - I think the PB here is very sweet to begin with and does not need much more - next time I will stick a chocolate square in the center (or Hershey's kiss) just to try it out.
These cookies are great and soooo easy!!  What a great recipe to keep, when your sweet tooth is callin!!  Thanks for posting.

loved this recipe... tho i did make a few changes.. i doubled the batch, did one cup granulated sugar, one cup brown.. added 2 tsp vanilla extract.. 1 1/2 tsp baking soda and 1/2 cup of all purpose flour to thicken.. it was really runny without it. oh yes! and milk chocolate chips! and it was faabulous.. my first time ever making peanut butter cookies and i am hooked.
love these, make them all the time!  sooo easy...was skeptical due to only 3 ingredients and the dough not seeming..."cookie-like", but they're awesome!
what an easy and yummy recipe.  I might try it next time with Xylitol instead of sugar, then it will be extra low in carbs since there is no flour. Yum!

I was a bit skeptical at first, but now I am a believer. These are delicious! Just what I needed to take away my craving for pb!
I added about a 1/4 cup of quick cooking oats, only used 1/2 cup of white sugar, and added 1 tsp of vanilla. 
I didn't pre heat the oven since it wasn't in the instructions and baked the cookies for 12 minuets.

I have never made peanut butter cookies before.  I don't know if they normally are harder, but I'll never find out 'cause this is how I'm gonna make em from now on!  I will also try with brown sugar and vanilla like one of the reviews says.  My husband loved 'em.. that's saying alot cause he doesn't normally like sweets.

Very convenient with minimal ingredients. Tasted good, but overall it was too sweet and a little too much peanut buttery taste for me. If somehow the thick taste of peanut butter can be lightened, this would be a five star.
These were great! My DH couldn't figure out why they were different, but decided he liked them anyway. Handed out at the Holidays, had a lot of compliments. Excellent recipe, thanks for posting.
I have made this recipe at home for years.  It is soooo good and easy.  I use 1/2 white sugar and 1/2 brown sugar, though.  Perfect with coffee.
INCREDIBLE!!!  The work-to-yumminess ratio is wonderful!

i loved these...everytime i have made peanut butter cookies,they have not turned out well...Peanut Butter Cookie recipes have been hit or miss with me and i havent found one that i liked until now.this is a great recipe,along with being simple..they taste great!!! I do agree not to let them get brown,they crumble when they get too dark.Great recipe,thanks for sharing!!!

The description of this recipe is quite right, they are definitely the quickest and simplest cookies I have ever made. I have found that if you put the dough in the fridge for about 10-20 minutes before rolling them into a ball, it's a lot less sticky. I also don't flatten down the middle of the cookies to give that peanut butter cookie design until after the cookies are already baked. There have never been leftovers when I've made these cookies...thanks so much for sharing!

I am in So much trouble. My cookie jar normally stands empty for months on end. I am admittedly not a baker ( but I am working on it). With this recipe I no longer have an excuse. Fast, Easy and who doesn't have PB, Sugar and eggs kicking around the kitchen.
I've been making this same cookie for years, with one small change of adding a teaspoon of vanilla extract.  So easy to make, but that seems to be a downfall too, since I always eat too many!
Fun, quick and easy. Also added vanilla, and used 1/2 splenda and 1/2 sugar. Couldn't tell the difference. Very peanut buttery!
Yum-O! I just got these out of the oven and they are great. My husband's family is coming over for Christmas and I needed quick, simple recipes for desserts and this just made my night!

These didn't even make the cool down.  They came out of the oven and were gone.  Thanks for a yummy and easy recipe. Will be making this again. For the Aussies out there I cooked these at 180 degrees and used standard sugar and mixed 1/4 crunch and 3/4 smooth peanut butter.

I chose only easy recipes for my holiday baking because my time was limited but I really wanted to get some baking done.  This recipe did not disappoint!  Seriously, three ingredients, 12 minutes in the oven, cool, have a nice peanut-buttery day.  ;)  I would hold this cookie up against other peanut butter cookies that require a lot more time, work and effort.  If you like peanut butter cookies because you like peanut butter, this is quick and easy.  (PS: I used a small tablespoon 'ice cream' scoop and this was as un-messy as I think you could ever get with a sticky peanut butter recipe!)  Awesome! Thank you so much!
So easy and hit the spot!  10 minutes almost seemed too long for my oven and it cooks slow.

I have made these cookies numerous times. Great for when I need gluten free cookies. I have also made them with splenda instead of sugar. Not quite as good as with sugar but a lot more weight watcher friendly.Still pretty tasty

I had my doubts, I'm picky about my peanut butter cookies.  But these are delicious off the pan, and a different kind of delicious the next day.  Really peanut buttery, and dense!  DELICIOUS!  Our first set is gone in less than 12 hours!  THANK YOU!
If you want a diet cookie, these aren't bad.  I used Splenda instead of sugar.  The only thing keeping these together is the egg, so they are very soft.  Not my favorite by a long stretch though.

These cookies were quick to make up, but are far from my favourite. I used half white half brown sugar and half Skippy (the in-laws bought it, I usually get the 'just peanuts' kind) and half 'just peanuts' peanut butter. The batter was fine and they cooked up fine; hubby likes them but I prefer my own recipe. These were, believe it or not, not salty-tasting enough. Very easy and quick, though.

These were pretty tasty. I wouldn't call them amazing---I generally prefer a chewy, tender cookie that's dense but tender, and much thicker than these---but they were nice, especially with milk.

I used reduce-fat peanut butter, which I don't care for. It worked pretty nicely in this recipe, though. I found that mine baked very, very quickly on the bottom, leading to either a done top and burnt bottom or a good bottom and crumbly, squishy top. They also turned out quite a bit darker than the ones in the recipe photo and tended to spread.

Great cookies, but they didn't really look anything like the picture. I suggest adding a little less sugar, and definitely not the extra sugar, because they are PLENTY sweet. I also suggest wetting the fork a little before you press down or they can ruin the shape of the cookies.
I've made 2 batches in the last week for 2 different people and both loved them. Gave the recipe to my mom and she made them and they loved them too.

Great quick recipe.  Short ingredient list was great for using stuff on hand. These aren't quite the same as the long ingredient list but delicious none the less. I recommend using crunchy peanut butter and a mix of brown and white sugar. Thanks!

I used chicken and served it over buttered noodles, with some greenbeans with parm cheese and it was wonderful meal.  DH kept saying "WOW".  Great flavors.  I don't usually change someone recipe, but did but only by accident. increased cayene to  3/4 tsp.  Definitely a keeper
THANKS!!!
This is exactly how I cook my ribs but I boil them for about an hour first to get them nice and soft.  This is the easiest way to make ribs and at barbecues everyone loves them.
these are awsome,i always boil my ribs too,but a bit longer to make sure they are cooked through,its easier to do this for bbq's too because you don't have to worry if they are done:)
I had this recipe for years before trying it.  Wow, was I missing out.  It is so simple and creates a tasty chicken dish that even looks beautiful.
Loved this dish.  It smelled wonderful while cooking and was so tasty.  Thanks for this recipe!  Will definitely use this again.

This is a great recipe.  I goofed when reading the directions and didn't notice the "crumb" section so I ended up putting some cocoa into the bread mix.  Another great way to use up zucchini from the CSA.

We loved this.  We have tons of chard growing in our garden this year and this was so simple, easy, and healthy.  What took the longest was harvesting and washing the chard.  I'll be adding this to my garden cookbook for sure!

I have also been looking for this recipe.  I made it up as soon as I found the recipe.  I decided to cook some of the cakes on my Gorge Forman Grill.  The grilled cakes are on the left, and the fried ones are on the right.  I preferred the taste of the grilled cakes.

Great recipe!  It takes a very long time to prepare but the yield is so huge and delicious that the hard work pays off.  My mom is Korean and so she was able to help hone the part after the dough is removed from the fridge.  The filling could have probably used a little less cinnamon since it was a little over powering.  Make sure that you flatten the dough pretty thin or else the outsides will be all dough.  Use a liberal amount of filling on each ho dduk.  When flattening the ho dduk in the skillet you are looking to get it to the thickness of about 1/2 inch or so.  These are definitely best hot off the skillet.  Thanks for publishing this gem of a recipe!
The Perfect Rice made perfect rice.  I used long grain and followed your directions.  Thanks,

I made this last year and I plan on doing it again. The idea of baking it in a tube pan is perfect. The bread bakes all the way through without drying out or browning the top and sides too much. I bought paper tube pans from KA and it was perfect for gift giving. For the dried fruit I used a mixture from Trader Joes that included dried cherries, cranberries, raisins and maybe blueberries and I did put additional fresh orange zest as well. It is well worth the steps. Can't wait to make it again. If you have leftovers use it for the most delicious french toast or a bread pudding.
This tasted great. Maybe not entirely convincing, but that's not a huge deal.  I might try processing the ingredients first though to make it more meat-like.
This was absolutely fabulous!  My family loved this dish, and I will be making this one over and over again.  Thanks for the recipe Derf!

I couldn't believe it! My children actually really liked this. My family ate it all. We had no leftovers. I have been requested to make this again. I used a little less onions than the recipe called for, I used oregano and pecans as my nuts. Very good, easy to follow and I'll be making this again.

My new favorite green bean recipe!  I made it with half butter and half olive oil, and dried thyme.  Even without the nuts, this was a definite winner, and my husband agreed enthusiastically.  I'm sure it would be even better with nuts.  Toasted for extra flavor, as my foodie son would suggest.) This went immediately into my personal notebook of winners, for regular use.  Thank you!

Yum! Hazelnuts are so wonderful with green beans! Make sure to toast the nuts first- it brings out their sweet nutty flavor. I skipped the cayenne and herbs and caramelized  the onions a bit. A great simple and quick side dish.

This is the best green bean recipe ever. My son, who hates all vegetables, had 2 servings and then came back to make sure I didn't throw the leftovers away. We had guests over for dinner an they raved about them. I toasted the hazlenuts (350 for 10 minutes)...definitely use hazelnuts. Substituted shallots for onions and used fresh thyme. I never knew how to properly cook fresh green beans until now. This will be a staple recipe for my family. I can honestly say I have never been excited about a vegetable side dish before now.
This got high marks at our house- my 10 year old requests now it any time we make waffles or pancakes!

i thought i already rated these...this was absolutely wonderful, it was like candy bacon, sweet, crispy and bacony!! i made it with french toast...this has already gone on my rotation..THANKS for posting!!
WOW! This is amazing, perfect for summer! (and no heavy cream!)
very good,thank you .i will keep this on hand.

This was okay.  I made it exactly as the recipe stated, and ended up adding the entire amount to 3 lbs of lean browned ground beef.  It made just under 6 Tbls.  The taste was pretty mild.  My husband said he could not really even taste the taco flavor.

Best.  Seasoning.  Ever.

I use this all the time for nearly all my mexican dishes.  It's great in tacos, of course, but I also sprinkle a bit on chicken when I am cooking it for fajitas, and I have used it as a rub for broiled steaks (that I then cut up) for fajitas as well!

Excellent mix.  I have made this 3 or 4 times now and can't believe I haven't left a review.  Like another reviewer I too use it for fajitas and I add a little lime juice and worcestershire sauce to the mix and then marinate the chicken, yummy.  Thanks Kittencal.
Yummy, my family and I really enjoy this and I have not used store bought since I tried this!

LOVE THIS!!!  I have used this now about 5 times on ground turkey, for turkey tacos!  I mix in approx., 1/2 cup of water after the meat is browned along with the seasoning mixture...let it simmer until I don't see the water anymore...PERFECT!
My family loved it!
My husband said " this is damn good Chicken" about our chicken tacos... enough said ;)

What a great taco seasoning.  I was out of my usual package so I whipped this up.  We were all pleased with the result.  I am thrilled to find something that I can make in my own kitchen without all the additives in the store-bought variety.  Thanks!

Better than the packets because it's not so salty that salt is all you can taste.  I added a lone packet of instant tomato soup I had on the shelf and it seemed to work well.  I'll be making this in batches and adding it to other things as suggested.
great taco mix, thanks for sharing your recipe

I'll be using this instead of buying the packets of taco seasoning from now on.  Mixed, it makes 1.5 ounces which is the weight of a packet of seasoning.  However, I expect that most of what comes in the store-bought packets contains mostly fillers and little spice.  I used about 1 TBSP. of this seasoning for 1 pound of ground beef, and I found it perfect in that ratio.  Another great recipe - thanks Kittencal!
Thank you SO much for this recipe. I use this every time I need a packet. My son has a reaction to the bought stuff and I can adjust this to suit us. Another winner and staple from you. Thanks!

This worked out great!   I subbed 1/2 teaspoon of salt substitute for the seasoned salt since I was making it for a meal that included someone on a restricted diet, and it tasted great!   Thanks for another winner, Kittencal!
I didn't like this too much.  I think the chili powder overpowered the cumin and other flavors.

I am very excited about this very versatile recipe! I am looking forward to putting it in every thing I make containing beef or venison in order to boost the flavor. I am glad Thunder Bay Laura gave the ratio of 1 T/1 lb. of meat. Now I won't have to experiment.I made 10x the amt. and put in the empty 13oz taco seasoning container that I had used up previously. 10x filled the container a little more that halfway which was perfect for me to be able to just shake and mix. This is the ratio I used: 1 1/4 c chile powder, 1/2 c cumin, 3 heaping T pepper, seasoning salt and garlic powder, 5 t paprika, 2 1/2 t onion powder and oregano. I left out the cayenne. I figure I can always add it to my cooking if I need it. Thx Kittencal! Love your recipes!
Easy, common ingredients, kids liked it, husband liked it, no unidentifiable artificial ingredients.  Thanks Kittencal!
Kittencal does it again! What can I say? Perfect. Used in #107570.

Great taco seasoning. I highly prefer this recipe over store bought package mixes because of the freshness of the herbs and the less salt. I use the full 3 tsp of cumin because it is one of my favorite seasonings. Thanks for sharing.
Delicious!  I recommend toasting the slivered almonds and sprinkling them on top when served vs mixing them in, but either way, this was great!

This is the best pumpkin bread recipe I have found thus far.  It's very moist without being undercooked.  That said, I had to cook the loaf for approximately 90 minutes.  I also tried this exact recipe, but substituted bananas, for a banana bread.  Unfortunately, that one didn't work out so well.

I really like the concept of this recipe, but the spices were a little intense--even with a doubled amount of zucchini.  I'd recommend making it with 2 zucchinis and maybe half the amount of garlic, pepper, and onion salt.  Either way, great way to get more vegetables in, and super easy to prepare--thanks for posting it.

Mmmm..we like zucchini and this has great flavor.  I made exactly as directed which was fine.  Next time I'm going to try substituting apple juice for the wine just to try it since we always have apple juice and sometimes the wine just slips away.  Also going to try julienne the zucchini since we have that in our freezer from our garden.  Thank you!

Fantastic recipe, although being new to bread making, I wasn't sure what to expect!  The dough will be very loose, soft, and sticky for those who do not know, and since it is a heavyish bread don't ball it too tight when you put it in the oven (I needed to cook it longer and gave it a crispier bottom for my trouble).  Otherwise, very easy to follow, very tasty, I live in a house with 19 other people who all got to havea taste, so a big 20 thumbs up!

Delish! I followed the recipe exactly only I added minced garlic to the butter before I added the wine/broth. I served the chicken over white rice where it allowed the liquid to absorb into the rice so we could enjoy the great tasting liquids. This recipe is definitely a keeper that I will make again!

The spaetzel are wonderful.  I never made 'em before, but used 6 eggs plus the sour cream suggested by a reviewer.  My colander has small holes and I couldn't see sitting there with a spoon for so MANY little noodles, so I put the batter in a quart-sized storage bag, cut one of the corners off, and squeezed the batter into the broth.  It worked!  And I did need to fish out the first half before the second half could reach the liquid.

Maybe because I only had regular paprika instead of smoked, the flavor of the broth was VERY bland, but with a very nice flavor.  I did add some extra paprika, but I'll try 3T next time.  I'll also salt and pepper the chicken before browning.

I was always afraid to make this dish because i thought it was difficult and involved a lot of patience and hard work! This recipe changed my mind. i have made it twice now and havent screwed it up! thanks soooo much. the family loves it and i like making it!!

My son is dissappointed we can't give this more stars!  I've made this several times and it is a big hit at our house. My mother used to make noodles this way and she called them "rivlets" and put them in soup so I used her method and just took the ball of dough and pinched off pieces, flattening them with my thumb and finger before dropping it in the pot. 
Tried it with regular paprika the first time and it was good but then I purchased the smokey kind and ohh what a difference. Yum Yum!
This was really good! The first step took quite a while though. Yummy foodee!
I'm Hungarian this recipe is right on.This is the way my whole family makes chicken paprikash.The Hungarians call our dumplings "nokedli"I also add more onion,It makes the gravy a little sweeter.

This dish was absolutely wonderful!  Thank you so much for posting it!  I did change it a little to suit my family's taste.  Instead of boneless chicken, I used skinless leg quarters and simmered them in citrus mixture in a large pot.  I pierced them, too, to allow the juice to absorb into the chicken.  My DH is a very picky eater, and he loved it.  He suggested more orange juice in it for next time.  Even my picky 2 yr old DD and 11 mo old DS enjoyed the chicken.  Again, thank you very much!

I absolutely loved this dish!  It was so easy to put together and the roast was incredibly tender.  I seasoned the roast w/salt & pepper, browned it and put it in a crockpot.  I also sauteed the onions & some garlic before adding them to the crockpot.  I used quartered yukon gold potatoes and instead of water or beer, I added chicken stock (would have added beef stock if I had it).  I put all of the ingredients into a crockpot on low for approx. 8 hours.  The house smelled great and we had a wonderful meal, thanks for the recipe!

Really enjoyable pot roast.   I also found the salt a bit heavy in the gravy, having missed the earlier notes.  I'd probably cut the onion soup mix in half next time and see how that goes.  Looking forward to another try.
Delicious recipe.
It was pretty tasty. I used a red bell pepper instead of orange. I added ham. My boys who are picky eater loved it.
Thanks!
Pretty good, but next time I think I'll leave out the sugar. IT was a bit too sweet for my tastes and I think the cream cheese and Krab would make it sweet enough.

OMG!!!!! i just got done mixing this up, havent even made the wraps yet but i couldnt resist a taste. I also did not add the sugar and it tastes wonderful!!! i havent chopped up the eggs yet and dont think i would use them in the future but i will use them now, im sure they would taste wonderful with or without:) i would probly even serve the mixture as a dip for veggies or crackers because there is soooo much. Thanks for a great mouth watering recipe..a zillion stars

A wonderful fail-proof recipe! I omitted the celery, used 1/2 tablespoon less sugar and added a teaspoon of Old Bay seasoning and a smidge of pepper. Instead of lettuce we opted for fresh spinach and a few slices of cherry tomatoes. It was quick as well as filling and will be a staple on my summer meal plan. Thanks for sharing!
This is one of the best banana breads ever! I took it to work with me and it was gone before midmorning! I used Archer Farms vanilla yogurt. I will definitely be making this again!
I used 2 cups buttermilk for the milk, and canola oil instead of butter.  The bread came out lighter than most whole wheat breads I've made, not too crumbly, absolutely delicious.

These are really, really good. I think they're even better than a Payday Bar. I mean, how can you go wrong with peanut butter and peanuts???? I did use fat free sweetened  condensed milk because that's all I had. Will make these again.

Quick, easy and very easy to make.  I did use the wet hands tip and found it very helpful.  I think I would use 8 oz's of salted peanuts for one of the peanut layers just for my own taste.  These went over huge at the potluck I brought them to.
Easy and good, i used a bag of choc/peanut butter flavor chips and it was soooooooooo yummy, thanks for the recipe.
Great recipe.  Pay Day was always one of my favorites but not so easy to find.  Thanks for a great recipe.
Great!!  Gonna try with butterscotch chips nexttime!!

I would give these ten stars if I could.  I made these as a part of my Christmas treat selection this year and they were a hit!  They don't taste exactly like a payday (not as tough nougat consistency) but they taste GREAT, nonetheless!  DH wasn't crazy about them, but he doesn't like Payday bars either.  He said they would be better with some sort of chocolate component.  I will most definitely add this to my collection and make time and time again.

These could not have been easier to make with such few ingredients and so quick. I used wax paper as suggested to press into the pan and it work great. Wonderful treat for my family as well as my parents who just loaned us their generator and radio with the latest storm in Northern Ca. Try it you'll like it.
